                  │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐                       │\n│  │           REPORTING VIEWS                │                       │\n│  │  • v_timesheet_daily_summary             │                       │\n│  │  • v_labor_variance (est vs actual)      │                       │\n│  │  • v_step_duration_summary               │                       │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────┘                       │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Key Concepts\n\n| Concept | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| **Timesheet** | A daily container for one worker on one project. Status: draft → submitted → approved/rejected. |\n| **Work Log** | A single time entry within a timesheet. Links hours to a specific BOM item, work order, or SOP step. |\n| **Context Injection** | The voice agent receives a short-list of open tasks for the current unit/trade, so it can match spoken items to database IDs. |\n| **Earned Value** | Comparing estimated hours (from BOM/SOP) against actual hours (from work_logs) to detect budget variance. |\n\n---\n\n## 2. User Roles & Capabilities\n\n### 🔨 Field Worker\n\nThe primary user. Logs time at the end of each day (or throughout the day).\n\n#### What they can do after implementation:\n\n| Action | How | Details |\n|--------|-----|---------|\n| **Voice time entry** | Tap 🎤, speak naturally | \"I finished the toilet in 105, took 2 hours. Painted the bedroom walls in 106, 4 hours.\" |\n| **Manual time entry** | Select unit → task → hours | Pick from dropdown of open BOM items, enter hours |\n| **Review daily timesheet** | Open timesheet for any date | See all logged entries, total hours, status |\n| **Edit draft entries** | Modify hours, notes, status | Only while timesheet is in 'draft' status |\n| **Submit timesheet** | Tap \"Submit\" | Locks the timesheet and sends for supervisor approval |\n| **View rejection reason** | See why timesheet was rejected | Can edit and resubmit |\n| **Check \"My Tasks\"** | View open BOM items assigned | See what's pending for today |\n\n#### Voice Entry Workflow (Step by Step)\n\n```\n1. Worker opens app → navigates to \"⏱ Timesheets\"\n2. Taps \"🎤 Voice Entry\"\n3. (Optional) Selects unit filter — e.g., \"Unit 105\"\n4. Taps \"Record\" and speaks:\n   \"I finished the rough-in for the master bath toilet\n    and the guest bath sink. Took about 4 hours total,\n    2 on each.\"\n5. App sends audio to API:\n   a. Audio → Whisper transcription → text\n   b. API fetches open tasks for Unit 105 (context injection)\n   c. Text + task list → LLM extraction → structured JSON\n6. App shows confirmation cards:\n   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   │ ✅ Toilet Rough-in (Unit 105, Master Bath) │\n   │    2.0 hours — Mark as \"Installed\"?       │\n   ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤\n   │ ✅ Sink Rough-in (Unit 105, Guest Bath)    │\n   │    2.0 hours — Mark as \"Installed\"?       │\n   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n7. Worker confirms → work_logs created → timesheet updated\n8. If ambiguous (\"Which toilet?\"):\n   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   │ ❓ Which toilet did you mean?             │\n   │    [Master Bath Toilet]  [Guest Bath]    │\n   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n   Worker taps correct option → resolved\n```\n\n#### Manual Entry Workflow\n\n```\n1. Worker opens timesheet for today\n2. Taps \"+ Add Entry\"\n3. Selects:\n   - Unit: \"105\" (dropdown)\n   - Task: \"Toilet Install — Master Bath\" (populated from open items)\n   - Hours: 2.0\n   - Status: \"Completed\" (optional)\n   - Notes: \"Flange was cracked, replaced it\"\n4. Saves → work_log created\n5. Timesheet total_hours auto-updates\n6. site_items.status auto-updates to \"installed\"\n```\n\n---\n\n### 👷 Supervisor / Project Manager\n\nReviews and approves timesheets. Monitors labor efficiency.\n\n#### What they can do after implementation:\n\n| Action | How | Details |\n|--------|-----|---------|\n| **View all team timesheets** | Filter by project, date, worker | See who logged what, when |\n| **Approve timesheets** | Tap \"Approve\" on submitted timesheets | Locks timesheet permanently |\n| **Reject timesheets** | Tap \"Reject\" with reason | Worker sees reason, can edit and resubmit |\n| **View labor variance** | Reports → Labor Variance | Compare estimated vs actual hours per BOM item |\n| **View daily summary** | Reports → Daily Summary | All workers' hours for a given day |\n| **View step duration** | Reports → Step Duration | Compare SOP estimated duration vs actual per step |\n| **Identify inefficiency** | Variance flags | Red flag when actual hours exceed estimate by >25% |\n\n#### Approval Workflow\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     Submit      ┌───────────┐     Approve     ┌──────────┐\n│  DRAFT   │ ──────────────▶ │ SUBMITTED │ ──────────────▶ │ APPROVED │\n│          │                 │           │                 │          │\n│ (editable│                 │ (locked,  │                 │ (final,  │\n│  by      │                 │  awaiting │                 │  feeds   │\n│  worker) │                 │  review)  │                 │  payroll)│\n└──────────┘                 └─────┬─────┘                 └──────────┘\n                                   │\n                                   │ Reject (with reason)\n                                   ▼\n                             ┌───────────┐\n                             │ REJECTED  │\n                             │           │\n                             │ (worker   │\n                             │  can edit │\n                             │  & resub) │\n                             └───────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n### 🏗️ System Administrator\n\nFull access to all data across organizations.\n\n#### What they can do after implementation:\n\n| Action | Details |\n|--------|---------|\n| View all timesheets across all organizations | Via sysadmin RLS bypass |\n| Access all reporting views | Labor variance, step duration, daily summaries |\n| Debug data issues | Direct access to timesheets + work_logs tables |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Data Flow: End-to-End\n\n### Scenario: Worker logs voice entry, supervisor approves\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant W as Field Worker\n    participant App as Site Companion App\n    participant API as Hono API Server\n    participant LLM as AI Agent (GPT/Whisper)\n    participant DB as Supabase (PostgreSQL)\n    participant S as Supervisor\n\n    Note over W,S: === VOICE ENTRY ===\n    W->>App: Taps 🎤, speaks \"Finished toilet in 105, 2 hours\"\n    App->>API: POST /voice {audio, project_id, unit_id}\n    API->>LLM: Whisper: audio → text\n    LLM-->>API: \"Finished toilet in 105, 2 hours\"\n    API->>DB: GET context/tasks (open items for unit 105)\n    DB-->>API: [{id: A1, label: \"Toilet Install\", location: \"Master Bath\"}]\n    API->>LLM: Extract: text + task context\n    LLM-->>API: {logs: [{site_item_id: A1, hours: 2, status: \"installed\"}]}\n    API-->>App: Confirmation payload\n    App->>W: Shows card: \"Toilet Install — 2hrs — Complete?\"\n    W->>App: Confirms ✅\n    App->>API: POST /timesheets (get-or-create for today)\n    API->>DB: INSERT timesheet (draft)\n    App->>API: POST /timesheets/:id/logs\n    API->>DB: INSERT work_log (hours=2, site_item_id=A1, source='voice')\n    Note over DB: Trigger: timesheets.total_hours = 2.0\n    Note over DB: Trigger: site_items[A1].status = 'installed'\n\n    Note over W,S: === SUBMISSION ===\n    W->>App: Taps \"Submit Timesheet\"\n    App->>API: PATCH /timesheets/:id/submit\n    API->>DB: UPDATE timesheets SET status='submitted'\n\n    Note over W,S: === APPROVAL ===\n    S->>App: Opens team timesheets\n    App->>API: GET /timesheets?project_id=X&status=submitted\n    API->>DB: SELECT timesheets + work_logs\n    DB-->>S: Shows: \"John — 2hrs — Toilet Install (Unit 105)\"\n    S->>App: Taps \"Approve\"\n    App->>API: PATCH /timesheets/:id/approve {action: 'approve'}\n    API->>DB: UPDATE timesheets SET status='approved', approved_at=now()\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Reporting Capabilities\n\nAfter implementation, the following reports are available through API endpoints and database views:\n\n### 4.1 Daily Summary Report\n\n**Endpoint**: `GET /timesheets/reports/daily-summary?project_id=X&date=2026-03-10`\n\n**Shows**:\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Daily Summary — Serenity Lane — March 10, 2026              │\n├──────────────┬───────┬──────────┬──────────────────────────┤\n│ Worker       │ Hours │ Status   │ Items Worked             │\n├──────────────┼───────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────┤\n│ John Doe     │  8.0  │ Approved │ 4 items across 3 units   │\n│ Jane Smith   │  6.5  │ Submitted│ 3 items across 2 units   │\n│ Bob Wilson   │  7.0  │ Draft    │ 5 items across 2 units   │\n├──────────────┼───────┼──────────┼──────────────────────────┤\n│ TOTAL        │ 21.5  │          │ 12 items, 7 units        │\n└──────────────┴───────┴──────────┴──────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### 4.2 Labor Variance Report\n\n**Endpoint**: `GET /timesheets/reports/labor-variance?project_id=X`\n\n**Shows**:\n```\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Labor Variance — Serenity Lane                                       │\n├──────────┬──────────────┬───────────┬──────────┬─────────┬──────────┤\n│ Unit     │ Item         │ Estimated │ Actual   │ Variance│ Flag     │\n├──────────┼──────────────┼───────────┼──────────┼─────────┼──────────┤\n│ 101      │ Toilet       │ 2.0 hrs   │ 2.5 hrs  │ +0.5    │ 🟡       │\n│ 101      │ Paint Walls  │ 7.0 hrs   │ 10.0 hrs │ +3.0    │ 🔴       │\n│ 102      │ Toilet       │ 2.0 hrs   │ 1.5 hrs  │ -0.5    │ 🟢       │\n│ 102      │ Flooring     │ 4.0 hrs   │ 4.0 hrs  │  0.0    │ 🟢       │\n└──────────┴──────────────┴───────────┴──────────┴─────────┴──────────┘\n\n🟢 On/under budget  🟡 5-25% over  🔴 >25% over\n```\n\n### 4.3 Step Duration Report\n\n**Endpoint**: `GET /timesheets/reports/step-duration?work_order_id=X`\n\n**Shows**:\n```\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Step Duration — WO-2026-042 (Toilet Install, Unit 105)               │\n├──────────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬─────────┬──────────┤\n│ Phase / Step     │ Est Duration │ Step Tracker │ Logged  │ Variance │\n├──────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────┼──────────┤\n│ Removal / Demo   │ 30 min       │ 25 min       │ 0.5 hrs │ 🟢       │\n│ Prep / Flange    │ 15 min       │ 20 min       │ 0.3 hrs │ 🟡       │\n│ Install / Set    │ 45 min       │ 60 min       │ 1.0 hrs │ 🔴       │\n│ Test / Leak Check│ 15 min       │ 10 min       │ 0.2 hrs │ 🟢       │\n├──────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────┼──────────┤\n│ TOTAL            │ 105 min      │ 115 min      │ 2.0 hrs │          │\n└──────────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────┴─────────┴──────────┘\n\n\"Step Tracker\" = duration_minutes from work_order_step_progress\n\"Logged\" = hours from work_logs linked via work_order_step_progress_id\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Integration Points\n\n### 5.1 Work Order Step Progress → Timesheets\n\nThe `work_order_step_progress` table already tracks `duration_minutes` per SOP step. After this implementation:\n\n- **work_logs** can reference a specific `work_order_step_progress_id`\n- The `v_step_duration_summary` view joins both data sources\n- This enables comparing:\n  - **Step-level tracking** (granular, per-step timer from the WO app)\n  - **Timesheet logging** (daily summary from the worker's perspective)\n\n### 5.2 BOM Status Auto-Update\n\nWhen a worker logs time with a `status_update`:\n1. `work_log` is inserted with `status_update = 'installed'`\n2. Database trigger fires\n3. `site_items.status` is automatically updated to `'installed'`\n4. `site_items.percent_complete` is updated if provided\n\nThis means **logging time also updates project progress** — no double data entry.\n\n### 5.3 Earned Value Calculation\n\nThe `v_labor_variance` view enables automatic earned value reporting:\n\n```\nEarned Value (EV) = percent_complete × labor_estimate_hours\nActual Cost (AC)  = SUM(work_logs.hours) for that item\nVariance          = EV - AC\n\nIf variance < 0 → Over budget (worker took longer than estimated)\nIf variance > 0 → Under budget (worker was faster than estimated)\n```\n\n### 5.4 Future: Payroll Export\n\nApproved timesheets contain all data needed for payroll:\n- Worker ID, date, total hours\n- Breakdown by project, unit, and task\n- Approval timestamp and approver ID\n\nA future payroll integration can query:\n```sql\nSELECT * FROM timesheets\nWHERE status = 'approved'\n  AND date BETWEEN '2026-03-01' AND '2026-03-15'\n  AND organization_id = :org_id\nORDER BY user_id, date;\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. API Endpoint Reference\n\n| Method | Endpoint | Auth | Description |\n|--------|----------|------|-------------|\n| `GET` | `/timesheets` | Worker | List own timesheets (filter by project, date, status) |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/:id` | Worker | Get timesheet with all work_logs + item details |\n| `POST` | `/timesheets` | Worker | Create or get-or-create timesheet for user+project+date |\n| `POST` | `/timesheets/:id/logs` | Worker | Add a work_log entry (manual or from voice) |\n| `PATCH` | `/timesheets/:id/submit` | Worker | Submit draft timesheet for approval |\n| `PATCH` | `/timesheets/:id/approve` | Supervisor | Approve or reject a submitted timesheet |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/context/tasks` | Worker | Get open BOM items for voice agent context injection |\n| `POST` | `/voice` | Worker | Voice recording → transcription → extraction → work_logs |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/reports/daily-summary` | Supervisor | All timesheets for a project+date |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/reports/labor-variance` | Supervisor | Estimated vs actual hours per BOM item |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/reports/step-duration` | Supervisor | SOP step estimated vs actual duration |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Database Schema (New Tables)\n\n### `timesheets`\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| id | UUID PK | |\n| user_id | UUID FK → auth.users | Worker who owns this timesheet |\n| organization_id | UUID FK → organizations | Tenant isolation |\n| project_id | UUID FK → projects | Which project |\n| date | DATE | The work day |\n| total_hours | DECIMAL(5,2) | Auto-calculated from work_logs |\n| status | TEXT | draft / submitted / approved / rejected |\n| submitted_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | When worker submitted |\n| approved_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | When supervisor approved |\n| approved_by | UUID FK → auth.users | Who approved |\n| rejection_reason | TEXT | Why rejected (if applicable) |\n\n**Constraint**: UNIQUE(user_id, project_id, date) — one timesheet per worker per project per day.\n\n### `work_logs`\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| id | UUID PK | |\n| timesheet_id | UUID FK → timesheets | Parent timesheet |\n| organization_id | UUID FK → organizations | Tenant isolation |\n| site_item_id | UUID FK → site_items | Link to BOM item (optional) |\n| work_order_id | UUID FK → work_orders | Link to work order (optional) |\n| work_order_step_progress_id | UUID FK → work_order_step_progress | Link to SOP step (optional) |\n| hours | DECIMAL(5,2) | Hours spent (0 < hours ≤ 24) |\n| status_update | TEXT | Optional status change for the BOM item |\n| milestone_reached | TEXT | e.g., \"Prime (30%)\" |\n| percent_complete | INTEGER | 0-100 |\n| description | TEXT | What was done |\n| raw_transcript | TEXT | Original voice transcript |\n| source | TEXT | manual / voice / ai_assisted / step_progress_sync |\n| notes | TEXT | Additional notes |\n| photo_urls | TEXT[] | Attached photos |\n\n---\n\n## 8. Frontend Pages (New)\n\n| Page | Route | Purpose |\n|------|-------|---------|\n| **TimesheetList** | `/timesheets` | Browse timesheets by week, see totals and status |\n| **TimesheetDetail** | `/timesheets/:id` | View/edit a single day's timesheet with all entries |\n| **TimesheetEntry** | `/timesheets/:id/entry` | Manual time entry form with unit/task selection |\n| **VoiceTimesheet** | `/timesheets/voice` | Voice recording with context injection and confirmation |\n\n---\n\n## 9. Security & Access Control\n\n| Table | Worker | Supervisor | Sysadmin |\n|-------|--------|------------|----------|\n| timesheets (own) | Read, Create, Update (draft only) | Read all in org | Full access |\n| timesheets (others) | — | Read, Approve/Reject | Full access |\n| work_logs (own org) | Read, Create, Update | Read all in org | Full access |\n\nRLS policies enforce:\n- Workers can only see/edit their own timesheets\n- Supervisors can see all timesheets in their organization\n- Sysadmins bypass all restrictions\n- Work logs inherit organization scoping\n\n---\n\n## 10. Glossary\n\n| Term | Definition |\n|------|------------|\n| **BOM** | Bill of Materials — the `site_items` table listing every physical task/item on the project |\n| **Context Injection** | Sending a short-list of open tasks to the AI agent alongside the voice transcript |\n| **Earned Value** | Comparing planned labor hours against actual hours to measure efficiency |\n| **Labor Factor** | Hours-per-unit multiplier from the taxonomy (e.g., 0.02 hrs/sqft for painting) |\n| **Milestone** | A progress checkpoint within a multi-stage task (e.g., \"Prime 30%\", \"Coat 1 70%\") |\n| **SOP** | Standard Operating Procedure — step-by-step instructions for a trade task |\n| **Step Progress** | Per-step completion tracking from `work_order_step_progress` |\n| **Work Log** | A single time entry linking hours to a specific scope item |\n| **Work Order** | A scoped assignment linking a template (with SOP) to a specific unit |\n\"\"\"\n\nwith open('/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/docs/workorders/TIMESHEET_WORKFLOW.md', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(workflow)\n\nprint(f'Written TIMESHEET_WORKFLOW.md: {len(workflow)} chars')"

> **Date**: 2026-03-09
> **Project**: voicelog-scaffold (Hennessy / Team Projects)
> **Codebase**: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold`
> **Stack**: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo

---

## Executive Summary

This prompt implements the bridge between the existing SOP library system and the existing Work Order (WO) template inheritance hierarchy. The goal is to let each WO template reference an SOP, expose SOP steps through the WO API, and track step-level completion/duration at the unit work order level — feeding a separate timesheet application.

The design principle is **surgical integration**: we add to existing files rather than creating parallel structures. The existing WO inheritance chain (system → company → project → unit) stays untouched. SOP linkage rides along automatically through the existing copy/freeze mechanisms.

---

## Context: What Already Exists

### WO Template Inheritance (DO NOT MODIFY)

The three-tier template hierarchy is fully implemented:

```
System WO Template (scope='system', org=NULL, project=NULL)
  └── copied to → Company WO Template (scope='company', org=X, project=NULL)
      └── lazy-ref → Project WO Template (scope='project', org=X, project=Y)
          └── (frozen when project activates)
```

**Key files** (read these first to understand the patterns):

| File | Lines | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `supabase/migrations/20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql` | 95 | `is_frozen`, NULL definition lazy-copy, `freeze_project_templates()` trigger |
| `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` | 1121 | 3 inheritance endpoints: `assign-to-company`, `assign-to-project`, `GET project/:projectId` with parent resolution |
| `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` | 415 | `copyTemplatesToOrg()` — copies system→company templates during onboarding |
| `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` | 746 | `WorkOrderTemplate` interface, `getTemplates(scope?)` method |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/ProjectTemplates.tsx` | 274 | Frozen/inherited badges |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateDetail.tsx` | 219 | Parent/copied-from links |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateEditor.tsx` | 531 | Frozen template warning |

**Key columns on `work_order_templates`** (already exist, some unused):
```sql
scope                   TEXT  -- 'system' | 'company' | 'project'
parent_template_id      UUID  -- company→project link (lazy-copy parent)
copied_from_template_id UUID  -- tracks origin
source_template_id      UUID  -- added in phase1, UNUSED
sop_id                  UUID  -- FK → sops, added in phase1, UNUSED ← WE WILL USE THIS
trade_id                UUID  -- FK → trades, added in phase1
inheritance_depth       INT   -- added in phase1, UNUSED
is_frozen               BOOLEAN -- set when project activates
definition              JSONB -- NULL = lazy-copy from parent
```

### SOP Library (EXISTS but disconnected from WO flow)

**Migration**: `supabase/migrations/20260228000001_sop_library.sql`

Existing tables:
```sql
sops                      -- master index (sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name, status, etc.)
sop_executive_checklists  -- cached checklist items (sop_id, phase_name, step_number, step_name, tasks)
sop_detailed_steps        -- cached detailed steps (sop_id, phase_name, step_number, detail_markdown)
sop_recommended_videos    -- video links per SOP
sop_sync_log              -- git→DB sync audit trail
```

**Phase 1 additions** (`20260304000001_schema_evolution_phase1.sql`):
```sql
sops.trade_id   UUID  -- FK → trades (exists)
sops.scope      TEXT  -- 'system' default (exists)
sops.outcome_id UUID  -- FK → project_outcomes (exists)
```

### SOP Authoring Workflow (docs-as-code)

SOPs are authored as markdown files following SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3:
- Location: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md`
- REF docs: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md` (shared per trade)
- Index: `docs/sop/SOP_INDEX.md` (master registry)
- Each SOP has: Executive Checklists (max 7 steps per phase) + Detailed Steps
- Phases are logical groupings: Removal, Installation, Prep, Apply, Finish, etc.

### Timesheet App (SEPARATE — do not build)

A separate application consumes `work_order_step_progress` data to build employee timesheets. We only need to provide the tracking table and API endpoints. The timesheet app will overlay duration data from step progress records.

---

## Implementation Plan


### Conversation 5 (Optional): Seed Data and Verification

#### 5A. Link existing SOPs to WO templates

One-time script:
1. Query all `sops` with `trade_code`
2. Query all `work_order_templates` with `scope='system'` and matching `trade_name`
3. Update `work_order_templates.sop_id` to point to the matching SOP
4. Verify linkage propagated to company-scope templates

#### 5B. Parse existing SOPs into new schema

Run updated `sop_manager.py` against all existing SOP markdown files.

#### 5C. End-to-end verification

1. System template has `sop_id` -> check
2. Company template inherits `sop_id` via `copyTemplatesToOrg()` -> check
3. Project template inherits `sop_id` via lazy-copy -> check
4. `GET /templates/:id/sop` resolves steps -> check
5. Create work order -> initialize steps -> update progress -> check
6. Step progress records have `duration_minutes` for timesheet app -> check

```", "summary": "", "tokens": 294}, {"_cls": "Message", "ai": false, "content": {"tool_name": "code_execution_tool", "tool_result": "workOrdersRoute.get('/work-orders/:id/step-progress', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const woId = c.req.param('id');

    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .select(`
            *,
            sop_step:sop_steps (
                id, phase_name, phase_number, step_number, step_name,
                tasks_summary, detail_md, est_duration_minutes
            )
        `)
        .eq('work_order_id', woId)
        .order('created_at');

    if (error) return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    return c.json(data || []);
});

// INITIALIZE STEPS from SOP for a work order
workOrdersRoute.post('/work-orders/:id/initialize-steps', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const woId = c.req.param('id');

    const { data: wo, error: woErr } = await supabase
        .from('work_orders')
        .select('id, template_id, organization_id, work_order_templates!inner(sop_id, parent_template_id)')
        .eq('id', woId)
        .single();

    if (woErr || !wo) return c.json({ error: 'Work order not found' }, 404);

    let sopId = (wo as any).work_order_templates?.sop_id;
    if (!sopId && (wo as any).work_order_templates?.parent_template_id) {
        const { data: parent } = await supabase
            .from('work_order_templates')
            .select('sop_id')
            .eq('id', (wo as any).work_order_templates.parent_template_id)
            .single();
        sopId = parent?.sop_id;
    }

    if (!sopId) return c.json({ error: 'No SOP linked to this work order template' }, 400);

    const { data: steps, error: stepsErr } = await supabase
        .from('sop_steps')
        .select('id')
        .eq('sop_id', sopId)
        .order('phase_number')
        .order('step_number');

    if (stepsErr || !steps?.length) return c.json({ error: 'No SOP steps found' }, 400);

    const { count } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })
        .eq('work_order_id', woId);

    if (count && count > 0) return c.json({ error: 'Steps already initialized', count }, 409);

    const records = steps.map(step => ({
        work_order_id: woId,
        sop_step_id: step.id,
        status: 'pending',
        organization_id: wo.organization_id,
    }));

    const { data: created, error: insertErr } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .insert(records)
        .select();

    if (insertErr) return c.json({ error: insertErr.message }, 500);
    return c.json({ initialized: created?.length || 0 }, 201);
});

// UPDATE STEP PROGRESS
workOrdersRoute.patch('/work-orders/step-progress/:id', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const id = c.req.param('id');
    const body = await c.req.json();

    const allowedFields = [
        'status', 'completed_by', 'started_at', 'completed_at',
        'duration_minutes', 'notes', 'photo_urls', 'override_detail', 'is_override'
    ];

    const updates: Record<string, any> = {};
    for (const field of allowedFields) {
        if (body[field] !== undefined) updates[field] = body[field];
    }

    if (updates.status === 'in_progress' && !updates.started_at) {
        updates.started_at = new Date().toISOString();
    }
    if (updates.status === 'completed' && !updates.completed_at) {
        updates.completed_at = new Date().toISOString();
    }

    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .update(updates)
        .eq('id', id)
        .select()
        .single();

    if (error) return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    return c.json(data);
});

// WORK ORDERS ENDPOINTS
// ============================================================================

-- Construction Management System Schema
-- PostgreSQL DDL

-- Enable UUID extension
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";

-- Enums
CREATE TYPE project_status AS ENUM ('bidding', 'active', 'archived');
CREATE TYPE item_status AS ENUM ('pending', 'ordered', 'delivered', 'installed', 'verified');
CREATE TYPE timesheet_status AS ENUM ('draft', 'submitted', 'approved', 'rejected');
CREATE TYPE product_availability AS ENUM ('in_stock', 'order', 'discontinued');

-- 1. Projects
CREATE TABLE projects (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    code TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    address TEXT,
    contact_info JSONB,
    status project_status DEFAULT 'bidding',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- 2. Project Definitions (JSONB Templates)
CREATE TABLE project_definitions (
    project_id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    taxonomy_version TEXT,
    floorplan_definitions JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- 3. Units (Inventory)
CREATE TABLE units (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    unit_number TEXT NOT NULL,
    floor_level INT,
    floorplan_key TEXT NOT NULL, -- Links to key in project_definitions
    specific_overrides JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, -- Unit-specific exceptions
    UNIQUE(project_id, unit_number)
);

-- 4. Site Items (BOM - Derived/Exploded)
CREATE TABLE site_items (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    unit_id UUID REFERENCES units(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    subject TEXT NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'toilet'
    location TEXT NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'Master Bath'
    action TEXT DEFAULT 'install_new', -- 'replace', 'keep'
    
    -- Status Tracking
    status item_status DEFAULT 'pending',
    percent_complete INT DEFAULT 0 CHECK (percent_complete BETWEEN 0 AND 100),
    
    -- Estimates
    labor_estimate_hours DECIMAL(10, 2),
    
    -- Metadata
    source TEXT DEFAULT 'original_scope', -- 'original_scope' or 'change_order'
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_site_items_unit ON site_items(unit_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_site_items_status ON site_items(status);

-- 5. Suppliers
CREATE TABLE suppliers (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    website TEXT,
    contact_info JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- 6. Products (Catalog)
CREATE TABLE products (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    supplier_id UUID REFERENCES suppliers(id),
    subject_key TEXT NOT NULL, -- Matches taxonomy subject
    model_number TEXT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    attributes JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, -- Spec matching
    unit_price DECIMAL(10, 2),
    product_page_url TEXT,
    image_url TEXT,
    notes TEXT,
    availability_status product_availability DEFAULT 'in_stock',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- 7. Project Supplies (Consumables)
CREATE TABLE project_supplies (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    supplier_id UUID REFERENCES suppliers(id),
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    product_page_url TEXT,
    image_url TEXT,
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- 8. Timesheets
CREATE TABLE timesheets (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL, -- Assumes external auth system
    date DATE NOT NULL,
    total_hours DECIMAL(5, 2) DEFAULT 0,
    status timesheet_status DEFAULT 'draft',
    submitted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    approved_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE
);

-- 9. Work Logs (Time Rows)
CREATE TABLE work_logs (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    timesheet_id UUID REFERENCES timesheets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    site_item_id UUID REFERENCES site_items(id), -- Link to specific BOM item
    hours DECIMAL(5, 2) NOT NULL,
    milestone_reached TEXT, -- e.g., 'Prime (30%)'
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_work_logs_item ON work_logs(site_item_id);

# Schema Design: Hybrid Relational + JSONB

## Strategy
We use **PostgreSQL** with a hybrid approach to balance performance and flexibility.
*   **Relational Columns**: Used for Foreign Keys, Indexing, Sorting, and Permissions.
*   **JSONB Columns**: Used for the deep hierarchical definition of the "Scope of Work", Attributes, and flexible metadata.

## Tables

### 1. `projects`
High-level container for a job site.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `name` (Text)
*   `code` (Text, Unique) - e.g., "PRJ-2026-001"
*   `status` (Enum: bidding, active, archived)

### 2. `project_definitions`
The "Brain" of the project. Steps away from relational rows to store the Template hierarchy.
*   `project_id` (FK)
*   `taxonomy_version` (Text)
*   **`floorplan_definitions` (JSONB)** - A dictionary of floorplans (`Plan A`, `Plan B`) and their default rooms/items.

### 3. `units`
The inventory of saleable/workable spaces.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `project_id` (FK)
*   `unit_number` (Text) - "101"
*   `floor_level` (Int)
*   `floorplan_key` (Text) - Links to `project_definitions`.
*   **`specific_overrides` (JSONB)** - Stores exceptions for *this unit only*.

### 4. `site_items` (The BOM)
Derived table. Exploded from JSON for tracking status.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `unit_id` (FK)
*   `subject` (Text) - "toilet"
*   `location` (Text) - "Master Bath"
*   `status` (Enum: pending, installed, verified)
*   `percent_complete` (Int)
*   `labor_estimate_hours` (Decimal)

### 5. `timesheets`
The container for a day's work.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `user_id` (FK)
*   `date` (Date)
*   `approval_status` (Enum)

### 6. `work_logs`
The link between Time and Scope.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `timesheet_id` (FK)
*   **`site_item_id` (FK)** - Links time to the specific BOM item.
*   `hours` (Decimal)
*   `milestone_id` (Text) - e.g., "Prime (30%)"

### 7. `suppliers` & `products`
The Catalog.
*   `suppliers`: Vendors.
*   `products`: Major items with `attributes` (JSONB) for spec matching and `image_url`.
*   `project_supplies`: Consumables list (brushes, tape).

# Timesheet Agent & System Contracts

You asked how the Timesheet Agent "knows" what to log against, and how the Timesheet App talks to the Core BOM System.

## 1. How the Agent Knows the Context

The Agent does **not** memorize the entire database. Instead, we use **Context Injection** (RAG-lite) at the moment of transcription.

### The Problem
*   User says: "I finished the toilet."
*   Database has: 500 toilets.
*   Agent needs to know: *Which* toilet?

### The Solution: "My Active Tasks" Context
When the user hits "Record" on the Timesheet App, the app acts as follows:

1.  **App Query**: The App asks the Core System: *"Give me the open BOM items for this User (assigned to Plumber) and this Location (Unit 105)."*
2.  **Core Response**: Returns a short JSON list:
    *   `id: A1`, `name: "Toilet Install"`, `status: "Pending"`
    *   `id: B2`, `name: "Sink Install"`, `status: "Pending"`
3.  **Prompt Injection**: The App sends this list to the LLM alongside the voice audio.

#### The Timesheet System Prompt
```text
You are a Time Tracking Assistant.
User Audio: "I finished the toilet in 105, took 2 hours."
Context (Available Tasks):
- [ID: A1] Toilet Install (Unit 105)
- [ID: B2] Sink Install (Unit 105)

Goal: Map the audio to a Task ID and extract hours.
Output: { "task_id": "A1", "action": "complete", "hours": 2.0 }
```

**Result**: The Agent doesn't need to know the whole Taxonomy. It only needs to pick from the *relevant short-list* provided by the API at runtime.

## 2. The System Contract (API)

The **Timesheet App** (Client) and the **Core BOM System** (Server) communicate via a strict API Contract.

### A. GET /api/v1/context/tasks
*Used by the Timesheet App to prime the Agent.*

**Request**:
*   `project_id`: UUID
*   `location`: "Unit 105" (Optional, derived from GPS or selection)
*   `trade`: "Plumbing" (Derived from User Profile)

**Response (The Contract)**:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "550e8400-e29b...", // The BOM Item ID (site_items.id)
    "label": "install toilet",
    "subject_taxonomy": "plumbing.toilet", // Helpful for the LLM to match synonyms
    "current_status": "pending",
    "milestones": ["Rough-in", "Trim-out"] // If defined in Taxonomy
  }
]
```

### B. POST /api/v1/timesheets/log
*Used by the Timesheet App to save the result.*

**Payload (The Contract)**:
```json
{
  "user_id": "uuid...",
  "logs": [
    {
      "site_item_id": "550e8400-e29b...",  // LINK TO BOM
      "hours": 2.0,
      "status_change": {
        "new_status": "installed",
        "milestone_reached": "Trim-out"
      },
      "notes": "Flange was cracked, replaced it."
    }
  ]
}
```

## 3. Handling Ambiguity (The Feedback Loop)

If the Core System returns *multiple* matching tasks (e.g., "Master Bath Toilet" vs "Guest Bath Toilet") and the user just says "Toilet":

1.  **Agent Response**: The Agent detects ambiguity based on the Context List.
2.  **Output**: `{ "error": "ambiguous", "options": ["Master", "Guest"] }`
3.  **App UI**: The App asks the user: *"Which toilet? Master or Guest?"*

This keeps the "Intelligence" grounded in the actual project data supplied by the Core BOM System.

        <>
            {!isSupabaseConfigured && (
                <div style={{ "background-color": "#e53e3e", color: "white", padding: "1rem", "text-align": "center", "font-weight": "bold" }}>
                    CONFIG ERROR: Missing Supabase Environment Variables. Check .env file.
                </div>
            )}
            <Router root={Layout}>
                <Route path="/" component={UnitList} />
                <Route path="/units" component={UnitList} />
                <Route path="/units/:id" component={UnitDetail} />
                <Route path="/voice" component={VoiceTest} />
            </Router>
        </>
    );
};

export default App;

import { Component, createSignal, Show } from 'solid-js';
import { Button } from '@repo/ui';

const VoiceTest: Component = () => {
    const [status, setStatus] = createSignal<'idle' | 'recording' | 'processing' | 'success' | 'error'>('idle');
    const [message, setMessage] = createSignal('');

    let mediaRecorder: MediaRecorder | null = null;
    let audioChunks: Blob[] = [];

    const startRecording = async () => {
        try {
            const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true });
            mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(stream);
            audioChunks = [];

            mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (event) => {
                audioChunks.push(event.data);
            };

            mediaRecorder.onstop = async () => {
                const audioBlob = new Blob(audioChunks, { type: 'audio/wav' });
                await sendToApi(audioBlob);
            };

            mediaRecorder.start();
            setStatus('recording');
        } catch (err) {
            console.error(err);
            setStatus('error');
            setMessage('Microphone access denied or not available');
        }
    };

    const stopRecording = () => {
        if (mediaRecorder && mediaRecorder.state !== 'inactive') {
            mediaRecorder.stop();
        }
    };

    const sendToApi = async (blob: Blob) => {
        setStatus('processing');
        const formData = new FormData();
        formData.append('audio', blob, 'voice_test.wav');

        try {
            const res = await fetch('http://localhost:3000/voice', {
                method: 'POST',
                body: formData
            });
            const data = await res.json();

            if (res.ok) {
                setStatus('success');
                setMessage(data.transcription || 'Success');
            } else {
                setStatus('error');
                setMessage(data.error || 'Upload failed');
            }
        } catch (e) {
            setStatus('error');
            setMessage('Network Error');
        }
    };

    return (
        <div class="p-4">
            <h1 class="text-2xl font-bold mb-4">Voice Verification Test</h1>

            <div class="flex flex-col gap-4 max-w-md">
                <Show when={status() === 'idle' || status() === 'success' || status() === 'error'}>
                    <Button onClick={startRecording}>
                        🎤 Start Recording Job
                    </Button>
                </Show>

                <Show when={status() === 'recording'}>
                    <Button onClick={stopRecording} style={{ "background-color": "red" }}>
                        ⏹ Stop & Process
                    </Button>
                    <p>Recording... (Speak now)</p>
                </Show>

                <Show when={status() === 'processing'}>
                    <p>Processing with AI... 🤖</p>
                </Show>

                <Show when={status() === 'success'}>
                    <div class="bg-green-100 border border-green-400 text-green-700 px-4 py-3 rounded">
                        <strong>Success!</strong>
                        <p>Task created: "{message()}"</p>
                    </div>
                </Show>

                <Show when={status() === 'error'}>
                    <div class="bg-red-100 border border-red-400 text-red-700 px-4 py-3 rounded">
                        <strong>Error</strong>
                        <p>{message()}</p>
                    </div>
                </Show>
            </div>
        </div>
    );
};

export default VoiceTest;

# Linking Timesheets to BOM Status

To verify status reports via timesheets, you need to connect the **Labor** (Timesheets) to the **Material/Scope** (BOM/`site_items`).

## 1. The Schema Strategy

We need to bridge the gap between "I worked 8 hours" and "I installed the toilet".

### `timesheets` (The Envelope)
*   `id`: UUID
*   `employee_id`: UUID
*   `date`: Date
*   `total_hours`: Decimal
*   `status`: 'draft', 'submitted', 'approved'

### `work_logs` (The Specifics)
This table links the time to the specific BOM items.
*   `id`: UUID
*   `timesheet_id`: FK -> timesheets.id
*   **`site_item_id`**: FK -> site_items.id (The BOM link!)
*   `hours_spent`: Decimal (e.g., 2.5)
*   **`status_update`**: Enum (Optional) - User can explicitly mark the item status here (e.g., "installed").
*   `notes`: "Had trouble with the seal."

## 2. The Workflow

### Step A: The Employee View (Mobile)
1.  **Select Unit**: Employee taps "Unit 105".
2.  **Select Task/Items**: App shows available BOM items for that unit (e.g., "Toilet", "Vanity", "Towel Bar").
3.  **Log Work**:
    *   Employee selects "Toilet".
    *   Inputs: "2 Hours".
    *   **Action**: Taps "Mark as Complete" (or inputs "50% Done").

### Step B: The Database Update (Backend)
When the timesheet is submitted:
1.  **Record Time**: 2 hours are logged against the Project/Unit for costing.
2.  **Update BOM Status**: The system automatically updates the `site_items` table:
    *   `site_items.status` -> "installed"
    *   `site_items.installed_at` -> Timestamp
    *   `site_items.installed_by` -> Employee ID

## 3. The Payoff: Automated "Earned Value" Reporting

Because you linked Time to Items, you can answer complex questions automatically:

### 1. "Is the project on schedule?"
*   *Calculation*: You have installed 50% of the toilets (verified by timesheets).
*   *Timeline*: You are only 30% through the timeline.
*   *Result*: **Ahead of Schedule.**

### 2. "Are we over budget on labor?"
*   *Estimate*: JSON says "Toilet Installation" = 2 hours avg.
*   *Actual*: Timesheets show average of 3.5 hours per toilet.
*   *Result*: **Over Budget.** The system flags this immediately, identifying *which* employee or *which* unit type is causing the variance.

### 3. "What did they do today?"
*   Instead of a generic "Worked on plumbing", the report says:
    *   "Unit 101: Installed Toilet (Complete)"
    *   "Unit 102: Installed Sink (Complete)"
    *   "Unit 103: Rough-in Shower (50%)"

## 4. Simplified "Task-Based" Logging
If selecting individual items (like "Towel Bar") is too tedious, you can group them into "Tasks".
*   **Concept**: Group all bathroom accessories (Towel bar, Ring, TP Holder) into a "Finish Hardware" task.
*   **Logic**: Employee logs time to "Finish Hardware".
*   **BOM Impact**: System updates *all* child items to "Complete" when the task is done, or distributes the % progress across them.

========== docs/timesheet/manual_admin.md ==========
# Admin Manual: Construction Management System

## 1. System Overview
This system uses a **Hybrid Database** approach:
*   **Structured Data**: For Projects, Units, and Products (easy searching).
*   **Flexible Data (JSONB)**: For the "Scope of Work" (Floorplans). This allows endless customization without asking IT to add columns.

## 2. Key Concepts
*   **Taxonomy**: The "Dictionary" of all possible work (Painting, Plumbing, etc.).
*   **Bill of Materials (BOM)**: The detailed list of every physical task/item on the site, generated from the Floorplan Templates.
*   **Context Injection**: The Agent "knows" the project because the specific list of open BOM items is sent along with the user's audio.

## 3. Sequence Diagrams

### Use Case A: User Logs Time (The "Happy Path")
User speaks, Agent matches speech to ID, System updates Status.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant App as Mobile App
    participant API as Core System
    participant LLM as Agent (AI)
    participant DB as Database

    User->>App: Taps Mic & Speaks ("Finished toilet in 105")
    App->>API: GET /context/tasks?unit=105
    API->>DB: Query open site_items for Unit 105
    DB-->>API: Returns list: [{id: "A1", label: "Toilet"}]
    API-->>App: Returns Context List

    App->>LLM: Send Audio + Context List
    LLM-->>App: Returns {task_id: "A1", hours: 2, status: "complete"}

    App->>User: Confirms "Mark Toilet Complete?"
    User->>App: Taps "Submit"
    App->>API: POST /timesheets/log
    API->>DB: UPDATE site_items SET status='installed'
    API->>DB: INSERT INTO work_logs (hours, employee_id)
```

### Use Case B: Issue Report (The "Change Order")
User reports a new problem, creating a Change Order.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant App
    participant LLM
    participant DB

    User->>App: Speaks ("Wall in 105 is damaged, needs skim coat")
    
    App->>LLM: Send Audio + Context
    Note right of LLM: Agent sees no existing task for "Skim Coat"
    LLM-->>App: Returns { type: "issue", subject: "walls", action: "repair" }

    App->>User: Shows "Report Issue: Wall Repair?"
    User->>App: Confirms
    App->>DB: INSERT INTO site_items (subject="walls", action="repair", source="change_order")
    Note left of DB: New BOM item created instantly
```

## 4. Managing Data
### Adding a New Floorplan
1.  Go to **Project Settings**.
2.  Clone an existing Floorplan (e.g., "Plan A").
3.  Add/Remove rooms or items in the JSON editor (or GUI).
4.  Click **"Sync BOM"**. This updates the `site_items` table for all unstarted units.

### Reviewing Timesheets
1.  Go to **Reports > Timesheets**.
2.  You will see rows like: "John Doe - 8 Hours".
3.  Expand row to see: "Unit 105 Toilet (2hr), Unit 106 Paint (6hr)".
4.  **Variance Flag**: If John took 6 hours for a 2-hour task, it will be highlighted Red.

# Field User Manual: VoiceLog App

> **Goal**: Use your voice to track work, report issues, and order supplies. Focus on the work, not the app.

## 1. Getting Started
1.  Open the App on your phone.
2.  Tap your **Project** (e.g., "Serenity Lane").
3.  Tap your **Unit** (e.g., "Unit 105").

## 2. Using Voice Commands
Tap the **Microphone Button** anytime to speak. Speak naturally.

### Logging Time (Daily)
Tell the app what you finished.
*   **Say**: *"I finished the rough-in for the master bath toilet and the guest bath sank."*
*   **Say**: *"Spent 4 hours framing the closet in the bedroom."*
*   **Say**: *"Completed the first coat of paint in the living room."*

### Reporting Issues (Change Requests)
See something wrong? Tell the app so we can fix it.
*   **Say**: *"The door hinge in the master bedroom is broken. We need to replace it."*
*   **Say**: *"The drywall in the kitchen has water damage. It needs a skim coat before we can paint."*
*   **Say**: *"The owner wants to add a dimmer switch in the dining room."*

### Ordering Materials
Need supplies?
*   **Say**: *"I need two boxes of 3-inch screws and a roll of duct tape."*
*   **Say**: *"Order a replacement vanity for the downstairs powder room, the white shaker one."*

## 3. Reviewing Your Work
After you speak, the app will show you a card summarizing what it heard.
*   **Green Check**: "Toilet - Installed"
*   **Text**: "Logged 4 hours."

If it looks wrong, just tap the **Undo** button and speak again.

## 4. Workflows

### Morning Routine
1.  Check **"My Tasks"** to see what's planned for today (e.g., "Unit 105: Paint Walls").
2.  Gather your materials.

### End of Day Routine
1.  Walk the unit.
2.  Tap the Mic.
3.  Say: *"I'm done for the day. Finished painting the walls in 105 and 106. Cleaned up the site."*
4.  Hit **Submit**. That's your timesheet!

import { OpenAPIHono } from '@hono/zod-openapi';
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
import { SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY } from '@repo/config';

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION: VOICE PROCESSING
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// This file serves as a reference for how to handle:
// 1. File Uploads (Blob)
// 2. Mocking AI Latency
// 3. Database Insertion
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export const voiceRoute = new OpenAPIHono();

// We need SERVICE ROLE to bypass RLS if the mobile app is unauthenticated (for the test).
// In production, you would use the User's JWT.
const supabaseAdmin = createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY);

voiceRoute.post('/', async (c) => {
    console.log('[Voice] Received Request');

    // 1. Parse Body (Multipart)
    // --------------------------------------------------------
    const body = await c.req.parseBody();
    const audioFile = body['audio'];
    // We expect a user_id to assign the task to, otherwise we can't notify the dashboard
    const targetUserId = body['user_id'] as string;

    if (!audioFile || !(audioFile instanceof Blob)) {
        return c.json({ error: 'No audio file provided' }, 400);
    }
    
    console.log(`[Voice] Processing Audio: ${audioFile.size} bytes`);

    // 2. Simulate AI Processing (The "Brain")
    // --------------------------------------------------------
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1500)); // 1.5s latency

    // Mock Result
    const extractedTask = `Voice Entry at ${new Date().toLocaleTimeString()}`;
    console.log('[Voice] AI Extracted:', extractedTask);

    // 3. Database Interaction
    // --------------------------------------------------------
    // BROADCAST STRATEGY (Test Mode):
    // Insert this task for EVERY user in the system so that whoever is looking at the dashboard sees it.
    
    const { data: users, error: userError } = await supabaseAdmin.auth.admin.listUsers();
    
    if (userError || !users.users.length) {
        console.error('[Voice] No users found to assign task to.');
        return c.json({ message: "Voice processed but no users found." });
    }

    const tasksToInsert = users.users.map(u => ({
        task: extractedTask,
        is_completed: false,
        user_id: u.id
    }));

    const { error: insertError } = await supabaseAdmin
        .from('testrepo_todos')
        .insert(tasksToInsert);

    if (insertError) {
        console.error('[Voice] DB Error:', insertError);
        return c.json({ error: insertError.message }, 500);
    }
    
    console.log(`[Voice] Broadcasted task to ${tasksToInsert.length} users.`);
    
    return c.json({
        success: true,
        message: `Voice processed and broadcasted to ${tasksToInsert.length} users`,
        transcription: extractedTask
    });
});

// ============================================================================
// STEP PROGRESS ENDPOINTS
// ============================================================================

// GET STEP PROGRESS for a work order
workOrdersRoute.get('/work-orders/:id/step-progress', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const woId = c.req.param('id');

    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .select(`
            *,
            sop_step:sop_steps (
                id, phase_name, phase_number, step_number, step_name,
                tasks_summary, detail_md, est_duration_minutes
            )
        `)
        .eq('work_order_id', woId)
        .order('created_at');

    if (error) return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    return c.json(data || []);
});

// INITIALIZE STEPS from SOP for a work order
workOrdersRoute.post('/work-orders/:id/initialize-steps', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const woId = c.req.param('id');

    const { data: wo, error: woErr } = await supabase
        .from('work_orders')
        .select('id, template_id, organization_id, work_order_templates!inner(sop_id, parent_template_id)')
        .eq('id', woId)
        .single();

    if (woErr || !wo) return c.json({ error: 'Work order not found' }, 404);

    let sopId = (wo as any).work_order_templates?.sop_id;
    if (!sopId && (wo as any).work_order_templates?.parent_template_id) {
        const { data: parent } = await supabase
            .from('work_order_templates')
            .select('sop_id')
            .eq('id', (wo as any).work_order_templates.parent_template_id)
            .single();
        sopId = parent?.sop_id;
    }

    if (!sopId) return c.json({ error: 'No SOP linked to this work order template' }, 400);

    const { data: steps, error: stepsErr } = await supabase
        .from('sop_steps')
        .select('id')
        .eq('sop_id', sopId)
        .order('phase_number')
        .order('step_number');

    if (stepsErr || !steps?.length) return c.json({ error: 'No SOP steps found' }, 400);

    const { count } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })
        .eq('work_order_id', woId);

    if (count && count > 0) return c.json({ error: 'Steps already initialized', count }, 409);

    const records = steps.map(step => ({
        work_order_id: woId,
        sop_step_id: step.id,
        status: 'pending',
        organization_id: wo.organization_id,
    }));

    const { data: created, error: insertErr } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .insert(records)
        .select();

    if (insertErr) return c.json({ error: insertErr.message }, 500);
    return c.json({ initialized: created?.length || 0 }, 201);
});

// UPDATE STEP PROGRESS
workOrdersRoute.patch('/work-orders/step-progress/:id', async (c) => {
    const supabase = getScopedClient(c);
    const id = c.req.param('id');
    const body = await c.req.json();

    const allowedFields = [
        'status', 'completed_by', 'started_at', 'completed_at',
        'duration_minutes', 'notes', 'photo_urls', 'override_detail', 'is_override'
    ];

    const updates: Record<string, any> = {};
    for (const field of allowedFields) {
        if (body[field] !== undefined) updates[field] = body[field];
    }

    if (updates.status === 'in_progress' && !updates.started_at) {
        updates.started_at = new Date().toISOString();
    }
    if (updates.status === 'completed' && !updates.completed_at) {
        updates.completed_at = new Date().toISOString();
    }

    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('work_order_step_progress')
        .update(updates)
        .eq('id', id)
        .select()
        .single();

    if (error) return c.json({ error: error.message }, 500);
    return c.json(data);
});

import { config } from 'dotenv';
import { resolve } from 'path';

// Load .env from project root (two levels up from apps/api/src)
config({ path: resolve(__dirname, '../../../.env') });
import { serve } from '@hono/node-server';
import { OpenAPIHono } from '@hono/zod-openapi';
import { swaggerUI } from '@hono/swagger-ui';
import { logger } from 'hono/logger';
import { cors } from 'hono/cors';
import { z } from 'zod';
console.log('[API] Zod Version:', (z as any).version || 'undefined');
console.log('[API] DOC_PARSER_URL is:', process.env.DOC_PARSER_URL);

// Import Routes
import { deliveryRoute } from './routes/delivery';
import { todosRoute } from './routes/todos';
import { voiceRoute } from './routes/voice';
import materials from './routes/materials';
import taxonomyRoute from './routes/taxonomy';
import projectDefinitionsRoute from './routes/project-definitions';
import unitsRoute from './routes/units';
import { workOrdersRoute } from './routes/work-orders';
import { documentsRoute } from './routes/documents';
import { a2aRoute } from './routes/a2a';
import { tradesRoute } from './routes/trades';
import { projectOutcomesRoute } from './routes/project-outcomes';
import { typeOrdersRoute } from './routes/type-orders';
import { bomActionRequestsRoute } from './routes/bom-action-requests';

import { reviewsRoute } from './routes/reviews';
import { entityCreationRoute } from './routes/entity-creation';
import { onboardRoute } from './routes/onboard';
import { docParserRoutes } from './routes/doc-parser-routes';

const app = new OpenAPIHono();

// Middleware
app.use('*', logger());
app.use('*', cors());

// Routes
app.route('/delivery', deliveryRoute);
app.route('/todos', todosRoute);
app.route('/voice', voiceRoute);
app.route('/materials', materials);
app.route('/taxonomy', taxonomyRoute);
app.route('/projects', projectDefinitionsRoute);
app.route('/units', unitsRoute);
app.route('/work-orders', workOrdersRoute);
app.route('/work-orders', documentsRoute);
app.route('/work-orders/trades', tradesRoute);
app.route('/work-orders/project-outcomes', projectOutcomesRoute);
app.route('/work-orders/type-orders', typeOrdersRoute);
app.route('/work-orders/bom-action-requests', bomActionRequestsRoute);

app.route('/reviews', reviewsRoute);
app.route('/entity', entityCreationRoute);
app.route('/onboard', onboardRoute);

// Doc-Parser Integration (P1-P7)
app.route('/doc-parser', docParserRoutes);

// A2A Protocol (Agent-to-Agent)
app.route('', a2aRoute);       // /.well-known/agent.json
app.route('/a2a', a2aRoute);   // POST /a2a (JSON-RPC endpoint)

// The OpenAPI Documentation JSON
app.doc('/doc', {
  openapi: '3.0.0',
  info: {
    version: '1.0.0',
    title: 'Construction Voice Platform API',
    description: 'API for Web Dashboard, Site Companion, and Flutter Mobile App'
  },
});

// The Swagger UI (Visual Documentation)
app.get('/ui', swaggerUI({ url: '/doc' }));

const port = 3000;
console.log(`Server is running on port ${port}`);
console.log(`Swagger UI available at http://localhost:${port}/ui`);

serve({
  fetch: app.fetch,
  port
});

export type AppType = typeof app;

    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

    section_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    section_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (section_type IN (
        'purpose', 'scope', 'references', 'personnel', 'safety',
        'tools', 'pre_work', 'procedure', 'videos', 'testing',
        'cleanup', 'troubleshooting', 'quality', 'revision_history'

-- 1I. RLS Policies
-- =============================================================================

-- sop_sections
ALTER TABLE public.sop_sections ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "sop_sections_select" ON public.sop_sections FOR SELECT
    USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');

    USING (public.is_sysadmin());

    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.sop_sections

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_sections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    section_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (section_type IN (
        'purpose', 'scope', 'references', 'personnel', 'safety',
        'tools', 'pre_work', 'procedure', 'videos', 'testing',
        'cleanup', 'troubleshooting', 'quality', 'revision_history'
    )),
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    content_md TEXT,
    content_plain TEXT,
    is_ref_pointer BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    ref_section TEXT,
    phase_name TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(sop_id, section_number)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_sections IS 'Structured sections parsed from SOP markdown files';
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_sections_sop ON public.sop_sections(sop_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_sections_type ON public.sop_sections(section_type);

--     Each row = one trackable unit of work = one RAG chunk
--     This is the table that work_order_step_progress references

-- 1D. work_order_step_progress — step-level completion tracking
--     The bridge table where field workers record completion of each SOP step
--     within a unit work order. The separate timesheet app reads this data.

# Agent Prompt & Database Insertion Strategy

This guide provides the specific System Prompt to parse user intent into the JSON structure, and the TypeScript code to apply that update to the database.

## 1. The Agent System Prompt

This prompt is designed to be sent to an LLM (e.g., GPT-4 or generic model) along with the user's transcription. It establishes the "Contractor Taxonomy" as the ground truth but allows for flexible attributes.

**System Prompt:**

```text
You are an expert Construction Scope Agent. Your goal is to parse a General Contractor's voice transcription into specific "JSON Overrides" for a project's floorplan.

### Context
- **Taxonomy**: You understand standard residential construction subjects (walls, cabinets, flooring).
- **Goal**: The user is describing changes to a standard unit layout (e.g., "Plan A Studio"). You must output a JSON object representing the *difference* (override) from the standard.

### Input Data
- **Project Structure**: (Injected dynamically)
  {
    "floorplans": { "Plan_A_Studio": { "base_type": "Studio" } }
  }

### Rules
1. **Identify the Floorplan**: Match the user's description to one of the defined floorplans (e.g., "Plan_A_Studio").
2. **Identify the Room**: Match the room name (e.g., "Kitchenette", "Main_Living_Sleeping"). If the user uses a synonym (e.g., "Kitchen"), map it to the correct key.
3. **Identify the Subject & Attributes**: Extract the specific subject. This can be an existing subject (e.g., "cabinets") or a NEW subject (e.g., "chandelier").
4. **Structure**: Output a `modifications` object keyed by subject.
   - Core Measure: `{ "measure": "unit_type", "quantity": number_value }`
   - Attributes: `{ "attributes": { "material": "string", "color": "string", ... } }` (Optional, for non-numeric traits)

### Output Format
Return ONLY the JSON object. Do not explain.

Example User Input:
"For the Plan A Studio, in the Kitchenette, let's just do 12 feet of cabinets, shaker style white. And add a chandelier."

Example Output:
{
  "floorplan": "Plan_A_Studio",
  "overrides": [
    {
      "room_name": "Kitchenette",
      "modifications": {
        "cabinets": {
            "measure": "length_ft",
            "quantity": 12,
            "attributes": { "style": "shaker", "finish": "white" }
        },
        "chandelier": {
            "measure": "count",
            "quantity": 1
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## 2. Database Insertion Code (TypeScript)

This function handles the logic of merging the new override into the existing JSONB structure.

**Key Logic**:
- **New Subjects**: Validated by the `...newOverride.modifications` spread. If "chandelier" is in the new object, it gets added.
- **Attributes**: The interface uses `any` or a flexible Record to allow arbitrary attributes (material, brand, color).

```typescript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';

// Define the shape of the Agent's output
interface ModificationValue {
  measure?: string;
  quantity?: number;
  attributes?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
  [key: string]: any; // Allow future flexibility
}

interface AgentOutput {
  floorplan: string;
  overrides: {
    room_name: string;
    modifications: Record<string, ModificationValue>;
  }[];
}

interface ProjectDefinition {
  floorplans: Record<string, {
    base_type: string;
    overrides?: {
      room_name: string;
      modifications: Record<string, ModificationValue>;
    }[];
  }>;
}

/**
 * Applies an override parsed by the Agent to the database.
 */
export async function applyProjectOverride(
  projectId: string,
  agentOutput: AgentOutput,
  supabase: ReturnType<typeof createClient>
) {
  // 1. Fetch current definition
  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('project_definitions')
    .select('definition')
    .eq('project_id', projectId)
    .single();

  if (error || !data) throw new Error('Project not found');

  const currentDef = data.definition as ProjectDefinition;
  const targetPlan = currentDef.floorplans[agentOutput.floorplan];

  if (!targetPlan) throw new Error(`Floorplan ${agentOutput.floorplan} not found in project`);

  // 2. Initialize overrides array if missing
  if (!targetPlan.overrides) {
    targetPlan.overrides = [];
  }

  // 3. Merge Logic
  for (const newOverride of agentOutput.overrides) {
    // Check if we already have an override for this room
    const existingRoomOverride = targetPlan.overrides.find(
      (o) => o.room_name === newOverride.room_name
    );

    if (existingRoomOverride) {
      // Merge modifications:
      // 1. Spreading newOverride.modifications adds ANY NEW KEYS (New Subjects).
      // 2. It also overwrites existing keys with new data.
      existingRoomOverride.modifications = {
        ...existingRoomOverride.modifications,
        ...newOverride.modifications,
      };
    } else {
      // Add new room override entirely
      targetPlan.overrides.push(newOverride);
    }
  }

  // 4. Save back to DB
  const { error: updateError } = await supabase
    .from('project_definitions')
    .update({ definition: currentDef })
    .eq('project_id', projectId);

  if (updateError) throw updateError;

  return { success: true, updatedPlan: targetPlan };
}
```

### Verification
1.  **New Subject Insertion**: `...newOverride.modifications` ensures that if the Agent sends `{ "chandelier": {...} }` and it wasn't there before, it is now part of the object.
2.  **Attribute Handling**: The `attributes` key in the JSON allows storing "tile", "hardwood", "red", "matte" without forcing them into a `measure` field. The `ModificationValue` interface supports this structure.

# Transcription to JSON & Database Design Guide

## 1. Transcription Example & Overrides

### Scenario
A General Contractor (GC) is walking a specific unit type ("Plan A Studio") in a new project. They realize this specific project uses a different kitchen layout than their standard "Studio" template.

### User Transcription
> "Okay, I'm looking at the Plan A Studio units here at Serenity Lane. We're basing this on our standard Studio layout, but there are a few changes. In the Kitchenette, we aren't doing the usual 20 linear feet of cabinets. It's much smaller here, let's just do 12 feet of upper and lower cabinets. Also, for the Living Room area, we're upgrading to engineered hardwood instead of the standard carpet, call it 250 square feet."

### Resulting JSON Structure
The Agent parses this intent into an **override** on the standard `Studio` floorplan.

```json
{
  "project_definitions": {
    "floorplans": {
      "Plan_A_Studio": {
        "base_type": "Studio",
        "overrides": [
          {
            "room_name": "Kitchenette",
            "modifications": {
              "cabinets": { "measure": "length_ft", "default": 12 }
            }
          },
          {
             "room_name": "Main_Living_Sleeping",
             // Maps to "Living_Room" type in the base definition
             "modifications": {
                "flooring": { "name": "hardwood", "measure": "area_sqft", "default": 250 }
                // "hardwood" might replace a generic "flooring" key or be a specific attribute
             }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## 2. Database Design Recommendation: Hybrid Approach

For storing this data, a **Hybrid Relational + JSONB** approach is recommended.

### Why not pure Columns?
The "Project Definitions" (floorplans, rooms, specific subject overrides) are highly hierarchical and variable. Normalizing this into tables (`project_floorplans`, `floorplan_rooms`, `room_subjects`, `subject_overrides`) creates a schema that is:
1.  **Rigid**: Hard to change if you want to add a new hierarchy level.
2.  **Complex to Query**: Reassembling a single unit's full spec requires 4+ joins.
3.  **Slow to Write**: Saving a project requires dozens of inserts.

### Why not pure JSON?
You still need to query high-level data efficiently (e.g., "Find all projects in Austin").

### Recommended Schema

#### 1. `projects` Table (Relational)
High-level searchable fields.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `name` (Text)
*   `address` (Text)
*   `status` (Enum: 'bidding', 'active', 'archived')
*   `created_at` (Timestamp)

#### 2. `project_definitions` Table (JSONB)
Stores the deep flexible configuration.
*   `project_id` (FK)
*   `taxonomy_version` (String) - To know which master taxonomy this follows.
*   `floorplan_definitions` (JSONB) - The content of `"project_definitions"` from your JSON.
    *   *Benefit*: You can query specific properties inside JSONB if needed (e.g., "Find projects using 'Plan_A_Studio'"), but mostly you load this blob to process logic.

#### 3. `units` Table (Relational + JSONB)
The inventory list.
*   `id` (UUID, PK)
*   `project_id` (FK)
*   `unit_number` (String)
*   `floor_level` (Int)
*   `floorplan_key` (String) - Links to a key in `project_definitions`.
*   `specific_notes` (JSONB) - For unit-specific one-off overrides (e.g., "Unit 102 has a broken window").

### Summary
*   **Columns**: Use for Identity, Relationships (FKs), and Sorting/Filtering (Status, Date, Address).
*   **JSONB**: Use for the **Payload** (The complex definitions of trades, areas, and measurements). This matches the document-oriented nature of the LLM output.

## 3. Hierarchy of Flexibility

The JSONB design allows for scope changes at three distinct levels, providing immense power without schema migrations.

### Level 1: Global Taxonomy (The Dictionary)
*   **Where**: `contractor_taxonomy.json`
*   **Scope**: All Projects.
*   **Usage**: Defining what a "Bedroom" *usually* contains.
*   **Change**: "We now want all standard bedrooms to track 'crown molding'." -> *Update the taxonomy area_type.*

### Level 2: Project Floorplan (The Template)
*   **Where**: `project_definitions.floorplans` (in DB or JSON)
*   **Scope**: All Units in the Project with that floorplan.
*   **Usage**: "In this specific building, all Studio apartments have Smart Thermostats."
*   **Example**:
    ```json
    "Plan_A_Studio": {
       "overrides": [
          { "room_name": "Main_Living", "modifications": { "thermostat": { "action": "install_new", "type": "smart" } } }
       ]
    }
    ```
*   **Impact**: Instantly applies to Unit 101, 102, 103, 104, and 105.

### Level 3: Unit Specific (The Exception)
*   **Where**: `residential_units[i].specific_overrides`
*   **Scope**: Single Unit Only.
*   **Usage**: "Unit 105 has a broken door hinge we need to fix, but the others are fine."
*   **Impact**: Only Unit 105 gets a work order for a door repair.

### Adding New Fields (Schema Evolution)
Because we are using JSONB, you can add arbitrary fields to any node at any time to capture new requirements.

**Example**: Tracking Budget Codes
You can simply start injecting `"budget_code": "GL-123"` into your modification objects.
*   **Old**: `{ "stove": { "action": "replace" } }`
*   **New**: `{ "stove": { "action": "replace", "budget_code": "CAPEX-2026" } }`

The database does not need a migration column. The Agent just parses it, and the Frontend displays it if present.

import { createSignal, createResource, For, Show } from 'solid-js';
import { supabase } from '../lib/supabase';
import { A } from '@solidjs/router';
import type { Unit } from '@repo/schemas';

const fetchUnits = async () => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('units')
        .select('*')
        .order('unit_number', { ascending: true });

    if (error) throw error;
    return data as Unit[];
};

export default function UnitList() {
    const [units] = createResource(fetchUnits);

    return (
        <div class="p-4">
            <h1 class="text-2xl font-bold mb-4">Renovation Units</h1>
            <div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4">
                <For each={units()} fallback={<p>Loading units...</p>}>
                    {(unit) => (
                        <A href={`/units/${unit.id}`} class="block p-4 border rounded shadow hover:bg-gray-50 transition">
                            <h2 class="text-xl font-semibold">Unit {unit.unit_number}</h2>
                            <Show when={unit.unit_type}>
                                <p class="text-gray-600">Type: {unit.unit_type}</p>
                            </Show>
                            <div class="mt-2 text-blue-600">View Scope →</div>
                        </A>
                    )}
                </For>
            </div>
        </div>
    );
}
