import { createSignal, createResource, For, Show, createMemo } from 'solid-js';
import { supabase } from '../lib/supabase';
import { useParams, A } from '@solidjs/router';
import type { Unit, UnitScopeItem } from '@repo/schemas';

const fetchUnit = async (id: string) => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.from('units').select('*').eq('id', id).single();
    if (error) throw error;
    return data as Unit;
};

const fetchScope = async (id: string) => {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('unit_scope_items')
        .select('*')
        .eq('unit_id', id)
        .order('zone', { ascending: true });

    if (error) throw error;
    return data as UnitScopeItem[];
};

export default function UnitDetail() {
    const params = useParams();
    const [unit] = createResource(() => params.id, fetchUnit);
    const [scope, { mutate }] = createResource(() => params.id, fetchScope);
    const [selectedTrade, setSelectedTrade] = createSignal<string>('All');

    // Derived trades list
    const trades = createMemo(() => {
        const s = scope();
        if (!s) return ['All'];
        const t = new Set(s.map(i => i.trade || 'General'));
        return ['All', ...Array.from(t).sort()];
    });

    // Filtered items
    const filteredItems = createMemo(() => {
        const s = scope();
        const trade = selectedTrade();
        if (!s) return [];
        if (trade === 'All') return s;
        return s.filter(i => (i.trade || 'General') === trade);
    });

    const updateStatus = async (itemId: string, newStatus: string) => {
        // Optimistic update
        mutate((prev) => prev?.map(i => i.id === itemId ? { ...i, status: newStatus as any } : i));

        const { error } = await supabase
            .from('unit_scope_items')
            .update({ status: newStatus })
            .eq('id', itemId);

        if (error) {
            console.error('Failed to update status', error);
            // Revert on error could be implemented here
        }
    };

    const StatusBadge = (props: { status: string }) => {
        const colors = {
            pending: 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-800',
            in_progress: 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800',
            completed: 'bg-green-100 text-green-800',
            verified: 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800'
        };
        const color = colors[props.status as keyof typeof colors] || colors.pending;
        return <span class={`px-2 py-1 rounded text-xs font-medium uppercase ${color}`}>{props.status.replace('_', ' ')}</span>;
    };

    return (
        <div class="p-4 max-w-4xl mx-auto">
            <A href="/units" class="text-blue-600 mb-4 inline-block">← Back to Units</A>

            <Show when={unit()}>
                <div class="mb-6 border-b pb-4">
                    <h1 class="text-3xl font-bold">Unit {unit()?.unit_number}</h1>
                    <p class="text-gray-600">Type: {unit()?.unit_type}</p>
                    <Show when={unit()?.floor_plan_image}>
                        <div class="mt-4">
                            <h2 class="text-lg font-semibold mb-2">Floor Plan</h2>
                            <img
                                src={unit()?.floor_plan_image!}
                                alt={`Floor Plan for Unit ${unit()?.unit_number}`}
                                class="max-w-full h-auto rounded border shadow-sm max-h-[400px]"
                            />
                        </div>
                    </Show>
                </div>
            </Show>

            {/* Controls */}
            <div class="flex flex-wrap gap-4 mb-6 items-center bg-gray-50 p-3 rounded">
                <label class="font-semibold">Filter by Trade:</label>
                <select
                    class="border p-2 rounded"
                    value={selectedTrade()}
                    onInput={(e) => setSelectedTrade(e.currentTarget.value)}
                >
                    <For each={trades()}>
                        {(trade) => <option value={trade}>{trade}</option>}
                    </For>
                </select>
            </div>

            {/* Scope Table */}
            <div class="space-y-4">
                <For each={filteredItems()} fallback={<div class="text-gray-500 italic">No items found.</div>}>
                    {(item) => (
                        <div class="border rounded p-3 flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between items-start md:items-center gap-4 bg-white shadow-sm">
                            <div class="flex-1">
                                <span class="text-xs font-bold text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wide">{item.zone} • {item.trade}</span>
                                <h3 class="font-semibold text-lg">{item.item}</h3>
                                <p class="text-gray-700">{item.action}</p>
                            </div>

                            <div class="flex items-center gap-2">
                                <select
                                    class="border p-1 rounded text-sm bg-white"
                                    value={item.status}
                                    onChange={(e) => updateStatus(item.id, e.currentTarget.value)}
                                >
                                    <option value="pending">Pending</option>
                                    <option value="in_progress">In Progress</option>
                                    <option value="completed">Completed</option>
                                    <option value="verified">Verified</option>
                                </select>
                                <StatusBadge status={item.status} />
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    )}
                </For>
            </div>
        </div>
    );
}

=== SOP_WO_INTEGRATION_PROMPT.md (first 100 lines for style) ===
# SOP ↔ Work Order Integration — Implementation Prompt

> **Date**: 2026-03-09
> **Project**: voicelog-scaffold (Hennessy / Team Projects)
> **Codebase**: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold`
> **Stack**: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo

---

## Executive Summary

This prompt implements the bridge between the existing SOP library system and the existing Work Order (WO) template inheritance hierarchy. The goal is to let each WO template reference an SOP, expose SOP steps through the WO API, and track step-level completion/duration at the unit work order level — feeding a separate timesheet application.

The design principle is **surgical integration**: we add to existing files rather than creating parallel structures. The existing WO inheritance chain (system → company → project → unit) stays untouched. SOP linkage rides along automatically through the existing copy/freeze mechanisms.

---

## Context: What Already Exists

### WO Template Inheritance (DO NOT MODIFY)

The three-tier template hierarchy is fully implemented:

```
System WO Template (scope='system', org=NULL, project=NULL)
  └── copied to → Company WO Template (scope='company', org=X, project=NULL)
      └── lazy-ref → Project WO Template (scope='project', org=X, project=Y)
          └── (frozen when project activates)
```

|---|---|---|
| `supabase/migrations/20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql` | 95 | `is_frozen`, NULL definition lazy-copy, `freeze_project_templates()` trigger |
| `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` | 1121 | 3 inheritance endpoints: `assign-to-company`, `assign-to-project`, `GET project/:projectId` with parent resolution |
| `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` | 415 | `copyTemplatesToOrg()` — copies system→company templates during onboarding |
| `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` | 746 | `WorkOrderTemplate` interface, `getTemplates(scope?)` method |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/ProjectTemplates.tsx` | 274 | Frozen/inherited badges |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateDetail.tsx` | 219 | Parent/copied-from links |

### SOP Authoring Workflow (docs-as-code)

SOPs are authored as markdown files following SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3:
- Location: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md`
- REF docs: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md` (shared per trade)
- Index: `docs/sop/SOP_INDEX.md` (master registry)
- Each SOP has: Executive Checklists (max 7 steps per phase) + Detailed Steps
- Phases are logical groupings: Removal, Installation, Prep, Apply, Finish, etc.

### Timesheet App (SEPARATE — do not build)

A separate application consumes `work_order_step_progress` data to build employee timesheets. We only need to provide the tracking table and API endpoints. The timesheet app will overlay duration data from step progress records.

# Earned Value & Granular Activity Tracking

You asked how to track multi-stage activities (Filling -> Sanding -> Priming -> Painting) when the original scope just says "Paint", and how to handle estimated hours.

## 1. The Strategy: Milestones, Not Rows

**Problem**: Creating 5 separate database rows for "Paint Unit 101 Walls" (one for each coat/step) creates 5x the data entry work for the user.
**Solution**: Use **Weighted Milestones** on a single Item.

### Implementation in Taxonomy
We added `milestones` and `labor_factor` to `contractor_taxonomy.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "walls",
  "measure": "area_sqft",
  "default": 350,
  "labor_factor": 0.02, // Hours per sqft
  "milestones": ["Prep (20%)", "Prime (30%)", "Coat 1 (70%)", "Coat 2 (100%)"]
}
```

*   **`labor_factor`**: The default multiplier. 350 sqft * 0.02 = **7.0 Hours Estimated**.
*   **`milestones`**: Defines the "Percent Complete" checkpoints.

## 2. Calculating Earned Value (EV)

When an employee logs time or updates status, they select a Milestone.

### Scenario:
*   **Scope**: Paint Bedroom Walls (350 sqft).
*   **Budget**: 7.0 Hours ($350 labor cost).
*   **Status Update**: Employee marks "Prime (30%)" as done.

**Calculations**:
1.  **Earned Value (EV)**: 30% of Budget = **2.1 Hours** earned.
2.  **Actual Cost (AC)**: Employee logged 3.0 Hours to get to this stage.
3.  **Variance**: 2.1 (Earned) - 3.0 (Actual) = **-0.9 Hours** (Behind efficiency).

## 3. Handling Overrides

The JSONB flexibility allows you to override these defaults per Unit or per Floorplan.

### Example: "Bad Condition" Walls
Unit 105 has water damage, so it needs more prep active.

**JSON Override**:
```json
"specific_overrides": {
  "walls": {
    "labor_factor": 0.04, // Double the hours (14 hours total)
    "milestones": ["Heavy Patch (10%)", "Skim Coat (30%)", "Prime (50%)", "Finish (100%)"]
  }
}
```
*   **Impact**: Ideally, the Agent parses "The walls in 105 are rough, need skimming" and applies this override automatically. Now your logic for Unit 105 is accurate to the reality of that unit.

## 4. User Interface Recommendation

Don't make the user type "Filling". Give them a **Progress Slider** or a **Stepper** in the app.

*   [ ] Prep
*   [ ] Prime
*   [x] 1st Coat  <-- User taps here
*   [ ] Finish

Tapping "1st Coat" automatically sets the BOM item `percent_complete` to 70%.

## 5. Advanced Milestones (Hours & Days)

You asked if milestones can be defined by **Hours** or **Days** directly. Yes, the JSONB structure supports complex objects for milestones.

### Absolute Duration Milestones
Instead of a simple string array, milestone definitions can be objects:

```json
"milestones_complex": [
  { "name": "Cure Stage 1", "duration_days": 2, "labor_hours": 0.5 },
  { "name": "Cure Stage 2", "duration_days": 2, "labor_hours": 0.5 },
  { "name": "Final Seal", "duration_days": 1, "labor_hours": 2.0 }
]
```

### Impact on Calculation
If you use this structure, your EV calculation changes from **% of Total** to **Sum of Completed Steps**.

1.  **Total Budget**: 0.5 + 0.5 + 2.0 = **3.0 Hours** Labor.
2.  **Total Schedule**: 2 + 2 + 1 = **5 Days**.
3.  **Status**: User marks "Cure Stage 1" Complete.
    *   **EV (Labor)**: 0.5 Hours (16%).
    *   **Schedule Progress**: 2 Days (40%).

This allows you to track "Time Efficiency" (Labor) separately from "Schedule Efficiency" (Days), which is critical for curing/drying processes where no one is working, but the project is "busy".

Aggregation views\n\n```sql\n-- =============================================================================\n-- 1E. Views for reporting and earned value\n-- =============================================================================\n\n-- Daily summary per user per project\nCREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.v_timesheet_daily_summary AS\nSELECT\n    t.id AS timesheet_id,\n    t.user_id,\n    t.project_id,\n    t.date,\n    t.status AS timesheet_status,\n    t.total_hours,\n    COUNT(wl.id) AS log_count,\n    COUNT(DISTINCT wl.site_item_id) AS items_worked,\n    COUNT(DISTINCT wl.work_order_id) AS work_orders_touched,\n    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT wl.source) AS sources_used\nFROM public.timesheets t\nLEFT JOIN public.work_logs wl ON wl.timesheet_id = t.id\nGROUP BY t.id, t.user_id, t.project_id, t.date, t.status, t.total_hours;\n\n-- Earned value: actual vs estimated hours per site_item\nCREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.v_labor_variance AS\nSELECT\n    si.id AS site_item_id,\n    si.unit_id,\n    si.subject,\n    si.location,\n    si.labor_estimate_hours AS estimated_hours,\n    COALESCE(SUM(wl.hours), 0) AS actual_hours,\n    COALESCE(SUM(wl.hours), 0) - COALESCE(si.labor_estimate_hours, 0) AS variance_hours,\n    si.status AS item_status,\n    si.percent_complete\nFROM public.site_items si\nLEFT JOIN public.work_logs wl ON wl.site_item_id = si.id\nGROUP BY si.id, si.unit_id, si.subject, si.location,\n         si.labor_estimate_hours, si.status, si.percent_complete;\n\n-- Step progress with duration from both sources\nCREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.v_step_duration_summary AS\nSELECT\n    wosp.id AS step_progress_id,\n    wosp.work_order_id,\n    wosp.sop_step_id,\n    ss.phase_name,\n    ss.step_name,\n    ss.est_duration_minutes,\n    wosp.duration_minutes AS recorded_duration,\n    COALESCE(SUM(wl.hours) * 60, 0)::INTEGER AS logged_duration_minutes,\n    wosp.status AS step_status,\n    wosp.completed_by\nFROM public.work_order_step_progress wosp\nJOIN public.sop_steps ss ON ss.id = wosp.sop_step_id\nLEFT JOIN public.work_logs wl ON wl.work_order_step_progress_id = wosp.id\nGROUP BY wosp.id, wosp.work_order_id, wosp.sop_step_id,\n         ss.phase_name, ss.step_name, ss.est_duration_minutes,\n         wosp.duration_minutes, wosp.status, wosp.completed_by;\n```\n\n#### 1F.     │  awaiting │                 │  feeds   │\n│  worker) │                 │  review)  │                 │  payroll)│\n└──────────┘                 └─────┬─────┘                 └──────────┘\n Data Flow: End-to-End\n\n### Scenario: Worker logs voice entry, supervisor approves\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant W as Field Worker\n    participant App as Site Companion App\n    participant API as Hono API Server\n    participant LLM as AI Agent (GPT/Whisper)\n    participant DB as Supabase (PostgreSQL)\n    participant S as Supervisor\n\n    Note over W,S: === VOICE ENTRY ===\n    W->>App: Taps 🎤, speaks \"Finished toilet in 105, 2 hours\"\n    App->>API: POST /voice {audio, project_id, unit_id}\n    API->>LLM: Whisper: audio → text\n    LLM-->>API: \"Finished toilet in 105, 2 hours\"\n    API->>DB: GET context/tasks (open items for unit 105)\n    DB-->>API: [{id: A1, label: \"Toilet Install\", location: \"Master Bath\"}]\n    API->>LLM: Extract: text + task context\n    LLM-->>API: {logs: [{site_item_id: A1, hours: 2, status: \"installed\"}]}\n    API-->>App: Confirmation payload\n    App->>W: Shows card: \"Toilet Install — 2hrs — Complete?\"\n    W->>App: Confirms ✅\n    App->>API: POST /timesheets (get-or-create for today)\n    API->>DB: INSERT timesheet (draft)\n    App->>API: POST /timesheets/:id/logs\n    API->>DB: INSERT work_log (hours=2, site_item_id=A1, source='voice')\n    Note over DB: Trigger: timesheets.total_hours = 2.0\n    Note over DB: Trigger: site_items[A1].status = 'installed'\n\n    Note over W,S: === SUBMISSION ===\n    W->>App: Taps \"Submit Timesheet\"\n    App->>API: PATCH /timesheets/:id/submit\n    API->>DB: UPDATE timesheets SET status='submitted'\n\n    Note over W,S: === APPROVAL ===\n    S->>App: Opens team timesheets\n    App->>API: GET /timesheets?project_id=X&status=submitted\n    API->>DB: SELECT timesheets + work_logs\n    DB-->>S: Shows: \"John — 2hrs — Toilet Install (Unit 105)\"\n    S->>App: Taps \"Approve\"\n    App->>API: PATCH /timesheets/:id/approve {action: 'approve'}\n    API->>DB: UPDATE timesheets SET status='approved', approved_at=now()\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. │\n├──────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────┼──────────┤\n│ Removal / Demo   │ 30 min       │ 25 min       │ 0.5 hrs │ 🟢       │\n│ Prep / Flange    │ 15 min       │ 20 min       │ Integration Points\n\n### 5.1 Work Order Step Progress → Timesheets\n\nThe `work_order_step_progress` table already tracks `duration_minutes` per SOP step. After this implementation:\n\n- **work_logs** can reference a specific `work_order_step_progress_id`\n- The `v_step_duration_summary` view joins both data sources\n- This enables comparing:\n  - **Step-level tracking** (granular, per-step timer from the WO app)\n  - **Timesheet logging** (daily summary from the worker's perspective)\n\n### 5.2 BOM Status Auto-Update\n\nWhen a worker logs time with a `status_update`:\n1. `work_log` is inserted with `status_update = 'installed'`\n2. Database trigger fires\n3. `site_items.status` is automatically updated to `'installed'`\n4. `site_items.percent_complete` is updated if provided\n\nThis means **logging time also updates project progress** — no double data entry.\n\n### 5.3 Earned Value Calculation\n\nThe `v_labor_variance` view enables automatic earned value reporting:\n\n```\nEarned Value (EV) = percent_complete × labor_estimate_hours\nActual Cost (AC)  = SUM(work_logs.hours) for that item\nVariance          = EV - AC\n\nIf variance < 0 → Over budget (worker took longer than estimated)\nIf variance > 0 → Under budget (worker was faster than estimated)\n```\n\n### 5.4 Future: Payroll Export\n\nApproved timesheets contain all data needed for payroll:\n- Worker ID, date, total hours\n- Breakdown by project, unit, and task\n- Approval timestamp and approver ID\n\nA future payroll integration can query:\n```sql\nSELECT * FROM timesheets\nWHERE status = 'approved'\n  AND date BETWEEN '2026-03-01' AND '2026-03-15'\n  AND organization_id = :org_id\nORDER BY user_id, date;\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. API Endpoint Reference\n\n| Method | Endpoint | Auth | Description |\n|--------|----------|------|-------------|\n| `GET` | `/timesheets` | Worker | List own timesheets (filter by project, date, status) |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/:id` | Worker | Get timesheet with all work_logs + item details |\n| `POST` | `/timesheets` | Worker | Create or get-or-create timesheet for user+project+date |\n| `POST` | `/timesheets/:id/logs` | Worker | Add a work_log entry (manual or from voice) |\n| `PATCH` | `/timesheets/:id/submit` | Worker | Submit draft timesheet for approval |\n| `PATCH` | `/timesheets/:id/approve` | Supervisor | Approve or reject a submitted timesheet |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/context/tasks` | Worker | Get open BOM items for voice agent context injection |\n| `POST` | `/voice` | Worker | Voice recording → transcription → extraction → work_logs |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/reports/daily-summary` | Supervisor | All timesheets for a project+date |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/reports/labor-variance` | Supervisor | Estimated vs actual hours per BOM item |\n| `GET` | `/timesheets/reports/step-duration` | Supervisor | SOP step estimated vs actual duration |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Database Schema (New Tables)\n\n### `timesheets`\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| id | UUID PK | |\n| user_id | UUID FK → auth.users | Worker who owns this timesheet |\n| organization_id | UUID FK → organizations | Tenant isolation |\n| project_id | UUID FK → projects | Which project |\n| date | DATE | The work day |\n| total_hours | DECIMAL(5,2) | Auto-calculated from work_logs |\n| status | TEXT | draft / submitted / approved / rejected |\n| submitted_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | When worker submitted |\n| approved_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | When supervisor approved |\n| approved_by | UUID FK → auth.users | Who approved |\n| rejection_reason | TEXT | Why rejected (if applicable) |\n\n**Constraint**: UNIQUE(user_id, project_id, date) — one timesheet per worker per project per day.\n\n### `work_logs`\n| Column | Type | Description |\n|--------|------|-------------|\n| id | UUID PK | |\n| timesheet_id | UUID FK → timesheets | Parent timesheet |\n| organization_id | UUID FK → organizations | Tenant isolation |\n| site_item_id | UUID FK → site_items | Link to BOM item (optional) |\n| work_order_id | UUID FK → work_orders | Link to work order (optional) |\n| work_order_step_progress_id | UUID FK → work_order_step_progress | Link to SOP step (optional) |\n| hours | DECIMAL(5,2) | Hours spent (0 < hours ≤ 24) |\n| status_update | TEXT | Optional status change for the BOM item |\n| milestone_reached | TEXT | e.g., \"Prime (30%)\" |\n| percent_complete | INTEGER | 0-100 |\n| description | TEXT | What was done |\n| raw_transcript | TEXT | Original voice transcript |\n| source | TEXT | manual / voice / ai_assisted / step_progress_sync |\n| notes | TEXT | Additional notes |\n| photo_urls | TEXT[] | Attached photos |\n\n---\n\n## 8. Frontend Pages (New)\n\n| Page | Route | Purpose |\n|------|-------|---------|\n| **TimesheetList** | `/timesheets` | Browse timesheets by week, see totals and status |\n| **TimesheetDetail** | `/timesheets/:id` | View/edit a single day's timesheet with all entries |\n| **TimesheetEntry** | `/timesheets/:id/entry` | Manual time entry form with unit/task selection |\n| **VoiceTimesheet** | `/timesheets/voice` | Voice recording with context injection and confirmation |\n\n---\n\n## 9. Security & Access Control\n\n| Table | Worker | Supervisor | Sysadmin |\n|-------|--------|------------|----------|\n| timesheets (own) | Read, Create, Update (draft only) | Read all in org | Full access |\n| timesheets (others) | — | Read, Approve/Reject | Full access |\n| work_logs (own org) | Read, Create, Update | Read all in org | Full access |\n\nRLS policies enforce:\n- Workers can only see/edit their own timesheets\n- Supervisors can see all timesheets in their organization\n- Sysadmins bypass all restrictions\n- Work logs inherit organization scoping\n\n---\n\n## 10. |\n\n---\n\n### File Locations\n```\ndocs/workorders/\n├── TIMESHEET_IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT.md   ← paste into new conversation\n└── TIMESHEET_WORKFLOW.md                ← user/team documentation\n```"

logs.content: **Hello! 👋**, I'm **Agent Zero**, your AI assistant. How can I help you today?

# SOP ↔ Work Order Integration — Implementation Prompt

> **Date**: 2026-03-09
> **Project**: voicelog-scaffold (Hennessy / Team Projects)
> **Codebase**: `/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold`
> **Stack**: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo

---

## Executive Summary

This prompt implements the bridge between the existing SOP library system and the existing Work Order (WO) template inheritance hierarchy. The goal is to let each WO template reference an SOP, expose SOP steps through the WO API, and track step-level completion/duration at the unit work order level — feeding a separate timesheet application.

The design principle is **surgical integration**: we add to existing files rather than creating parallel structures. The existing WO inheritance chain (system → company → project → unit) stays untouched. SOP linkage rides along automatically through the existing copy/freeze mechanisms.

---

## Context: What Already Exists

### WO Template Inheritance (DO NOT MODIFY)

The three-tier template hierarchy is fully implemented:

```
System WO Template (scope='system', org=NULL, project=NULL)
  └── copied to → Company WO Template (scope='company', org=X, project=NULL)
      └── lazy-ref → Project WO Template (scope='project', org=X, project=Y)
          └── (frozen when project activates)
```

**Key files** (read these first to understand the patterns):

| File | Lines | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `supabase/migrations/20260222000002_project_lifecycle_template_inheritance.sql` | 95 | `is_frozen`, NULL definition lazy-copy, `freeze_project_templates()` trigger |
| `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` | 1121 | 3 inheritance endpoints: `assign-to-company`, `assign-to-project`, `GET project/:projectId` with parent resolution |
| `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` | 415 | `copyTemplatesToOrg()` — copies system→company templates during onboarding |
| `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` | 746 | `WorkOrderTemplate` interface, `getTemplates(scope?)` method |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/ProjectTemplates.tsx` | 274 | Frozen/inherited badges |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateDetail.tsx` | 219 | Parent/copied-from links |
| `apps/work-orders/src/pages/TemplateEditor.tsx` | 531 | Frozen template warning |

**Key columns on `work_order_templates`** (already exist, some unused):
```sql
scope                   TEXT  -- 'system' | 'company' | 'project'
parent_template_id      UUID  -- company→project link (lazy-copy parent)
copied_from_template_id UUID  -- tracks origin
source_template_id      UUID  -- added in phase1, UNUSED
sop_id                  UUID  -- FK → sops, added in phase1, UNUSED ← WE WILL USE THIS
trade_id                UUID  -- FK → trades, added in phase1
inheritance_depth       INT   -- added in phase1, UNUSED
is_frozen               BOOLEAN -- set when project activates
definition              JSONB -- NULL = lazy-copy from parent
```

### SOP Library (EXISTS but disconnected from WO flow)

**Migration**: `supabase/migrations/20260228000001_sop_library.sql`

Existing tables:
```sql
sops                      -- master index (sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name, status, etc.)
sop_executive_checklists  -- cached checklist items (sop_id, phase_name, step_number, step_name, tasks)
sop_detailed_steps        -- cached detailed steps (sop_id, phase_name, step_number, detail_markdown)
sop_recommended_videos    -- video links per SOP
sop_sync_log              -- git→DB sync audit trail
```

**Phase 1 additions** (`20260304000001_schema_evolution_phase1.sql`):
```sql
sops.trade_id   UUID  -- FK → trades (exists)
sops.scope      TEXT  -- 'system' default (exists)
sops.outcome_id UUID  -- FK → project_outcomes (exists)
```

### SOP Authoring Workflow (docs-as-code)

SOPs are authored as markdown files following SOP-META-001 Rev 1.3:
- Location: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md`
- REF docs: `docs/sop/{TRADE}/REF_{TRADE}_Codes_Standards.md` (shared per trade)
- Index: `docs/sop/SOP_INDEX.md` (master registry)
- Each SOP has: Executive Checklists (max 7 steps per phase) + Detailed Steps
- Phases are logical groupings: Removal, Installation, Prep, Apply, Finish, etc.

### Timesheet App (SEPARATE — do not build)

A separate application consumes `work_order_step_progress` data to build employee timesheets. We only need to provide the tracking table and API endpoints. The timesheet app will overlay duration data from step progress records.

---

## Implementation Plan


### Conversation 5 (Optional): Seed Data and Verification

#### 5A. Link existing SOPs to WO templates

One-time script:
1. Query all `sops` with `trade_code`
2. Query all `work_order_templates` with `scope='system'` and matching `trade_name`
3. Update `work_order_templates.sop_id` to point to the matching SOP
4. Verify linkage propagated to company-scope templates

#### 5B. Parse existing SOPs into new schema

Run updated `sop_manager.py` against all existing SOP markdown files.

#### 5C. End-to-end verification

1. System template has `sop_id` -> check
2. Company template inherits `sop_id` via `copyTemplatesToOrg()` -> check
3. Project template inherits `sop_id` via lazy-copy -> check
4. `GET /templates/:id/sop` resolves steps -> check
5. Create work order -> initialize steps -> update progress -> check
6. Step progress records have `duration_minutes` for timesheet app -> check

---

kvps.memories:
Tech stack for voicelog-scaffold: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo.

Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview (Hennessy / Team Projects):

Location:
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold

Context & Identity:
- The project 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') served as the initial development seed.
- Primary Database Tenant: 'Team Projects'.

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Structure: pnpm monorepo managed with Turbo.
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005). Includes 'tenant-portal' with routes for /tenants, /tenant/:id, and /onboard.
- API: Hono (Port 3000). Located in apps/api, handling onboarding, organization management, and trade template assignment.
- Database/Auth: Supabase (PostgreSQL). Uses a local development environment (http://127.0.0.1:54321).
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL (Port 54322), PostgREST, and Hono API layer.

Key Systems:
- Tenant Onboarding: Full-stack implementation. The /onboard form and Tenant Detail page include industry selection (sourced from trades template table) and description fields. Redirects to work orders login (Port 3004) after success.
- API Endpoints: POST/GET /onboard, GET /onboard/trades, GET/PATCH /onboard/:id, and admin management.
- Database Management: Uses Supabase migrations via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate --local'.

Work Order (WO) template hierarchy and inheritance system:

1. Hierarchy Tiers:
- System/App Scope (scope='system' or 'app'): Master templates defined in 'supabase/seed.sql'. ID prefix '30000000'.
- Company Scope (scope='company'): Created during onboarding or industry updates. ID prefix '20000000'.
- Project Scope (scope='project'): Specific to individual projects. Templates are frozen (is_frozen=true) when a project activates, copying definitions from parent company templates.

2. Management & Inheritance:
- The onboarding system (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) manages templates via the 'work_order_templates' table.
- Industry updates trigger reassignment by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying from app-scope masters.
- Selecting 'General Contractor' assigns all app-scope templates; specific trades assign only matching ones.

3. API & Implementation:
- API/Client: 'GET /templates' and 'ApiClient.getTemplates' support an optional 'scope' query parameter ('app', 'company', or 'project').
- Filtering: The Dashboard component filters for 'company' scope to prevent duplicates.
- ID Logic: Uses 'a0000000' prefix logic for seeding app-scope from company-scope.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implement and verify a complex database migration (e.g., SOP/Work Order bridge) in a Supabase/PostgreSQL environment, ensuring schema integrity, RLS security, and cache synchronization.

# Solution

### 1. Migration Implementation
1. **Schema Definition**: Create migration files (e.g., `supabase/migrations/[timestamp]_name.sql`) defining tables (e.g., `sop_sections`, `sop_steps`), unique constraints, and foreign keys with appropriate `ON DELETE` behaviors.
2. **Logic & Triggers**: Update trigger functions (e.g., `freeze_project_templates`) to handle new logic and attach `updated_at` triggers to all new tables.
3. **Security**: Apply Row Level Security (RLS) policies for `authenticated` roles and organization-based restrictions.
4. **Cache Refresh**: Include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` at the end of the script to refresh the PostgREST cache.

### 2. Post-Migration Verification Suite
Execute these queries via `psql` or the Supabase SQL Editor to confirm successful application:
- **Tables & Columns**: Check `information_schema.tables` for new table names and expected column counts.
- **Indexes**: Query `pg_indexes` where `schemaname = 'public'` to verify performance optimization.
- **RLS Status**: Query `pg_class` for `relname` and `relrowsecurity` to ensure RLS is enabled ('t').
- **RLS Policies**: Query `pg_policies` for `tablename`, `policyname`, and `cmd` to confirm access rules match requirements.
- **Triggers**: Query `information_schema.triggers` for `event_object_table` and `trigger_name` to ensure automation is active.
- **Function Logic**: Query `information_schema.routines` and check `routine_definition` using `LIKE` to ensure specific logic (e.g., new column handling) was correctly injected into existing functions.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase. A specific issue was identified where the work orders dashboard showed duplicate trade templates (e.g., 'Painting' appearing multiple times) because the API returned templates from all scopes (app, company, and project) simultaneously.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Scoped Template Retrieval**: Modify the API handler in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` for `GET /templates` to accept an optional `scope` query parameter and apply it to the Supabase query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.
- **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
- **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
- **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **API Client Update**: Update the frontend API client in `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` to support the scope parameter: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? "?scope=${scope}" : ''; return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>( "/templates${query}"); }`.
- **Dashboard Component**: Update `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` to specifically request company-scope templates: `const [templates] = createResource<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Scoped Query Logic:**
```typescript
workOrdersRoute.get('/templates', async (c) => {
    const scope = c.req.query('scope');
    let query = supabase.from('work_order_templates').select('*').eq('is_active', true);
    if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }
    const { data } = await query.order('trade_name');
    return c.json(data);
});
```

**Frontend API Client:**
```typescript
async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') {
    const query = scope ? `?scope=${scope}` : '';
    return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(`/templates${query}`);
}
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Duplicate Dashboard Entries**: Caused by API returning all scopes. Ensure the frontend explicitly passes `?scope=company`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.

```json
{

## Status Legend

| Icon | Status | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| ✅ | Active | Published, reviewed, ready for use |
| 📋 | Planned | Identified as needed, not yet authored |
| 🔄 | In Review | Drafted, pending review/approval |
| ⚠️ | Deprecated | Superseded by newer SOP |
| ❌ | Retired | No longer applicable |

---

## Adding a New SOP

1. Assign the next available number for the trade: `{TRADE}-SOP-{NNN}`
2. Author the SOP following `SOP_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md`
3. Save as `SOP_{TRADE}_{Task_Name}.md` using 3-letter trade code (e.g., `SOP_PLB_Sink_Replacement.md`)
4. Add a row to the **Full SOP Registry** table above
5. Update the **Quick Reference** count
6. Update the **Dependency Map** if the SOP links to others
7. Set status to 🔄 In Review until approved

---

| 1.3 | 2026-03-08 | — | Added Reference Document Registry section; registered REF-PNT-001 |

logs.heading: 3 memories and 2 relevant solutions found

kvps.memories:
Tech stack for voicelog-scaffold: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo.

Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview (Hennessy / Team Projects):

Location:
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold

Context & Identity:
- The project 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') served as the initial development seed.
- Primary Database Tenant: 'Team Projects'.

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Structure: pnpm monorepo managed with Turbo.
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005). Includes 'tenant-portal' with routes for /tenants, /tenant/:id, and /onboard.
- API: Hono (Port 3000). Located in apps/api, handling onboarding, organization management, and trade template assignment.
- Database/Auth: Supabase (PostgreSQL). Uses a local development environment (http://127.0.0.1:54321).
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL (Port 54322), PostgREST, and Hono API layer.

Key Systems:
- Tenant Onboarding: Full-stack implementation. The /onboard form and Tenant Detail page include industry selection (sourced from trades template table) and description fields. Redirects to work orders login (Port 3004) after success.
- API Endpoints: POST/GET /onboard, GET /onboard/trades, GET/PATCH /onboard/:id, and admin management.
- Database Management: Uses Supabase migrations via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate --local'.

Work Order (WO) template hierarchy and inheritance system:

1. Hierarchy Tiers:
- System/App Scope (scope='system' or 'app'): Master templates defined in 'supabase/seed.sql'. ID prefix '30000000'.
- Company Scope (scope='company'): Created during onboarding or industry updates. ID prefix '20000000'.
- Project Scope (scope='project'): Specific to individual projects. Templates are frozen (is_frozen=true) when a project activates, copying definitions from parent company templates.

2. Management & Inheritance:
- The onboarding system (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) manages templates via the 'work_order_templates' table.
- Industry updates trigger reassignment by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying from app-scope masters.
- Selecting 'General Contractor' assigns all app-scope templates; specific trades assign only matching ones.

3. API & Implementation:
- API/Client: 'GET /templates' and 'ApiClient.getTemplates' support an optional 'scope' query parameter ('app', 'company', or 'project').
- Filtering: The Dashboard component filters for 'company' scope to prevent duplicates.
- ID Logic: Uses 'a0000000' prefix logic for seeding app-scope from company-scope.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implement and verify a complex database migration (e.g., SOP/Work Order bridge) in a Supabase/PostgreSQL environment, ensuring schema integrity, RLS security, and cache synchronization.

# Solution

### 1. Migration Implementation
1. **Schema Definition**: Create migration files (e.g., `supabase/migrations/[timestamp]_name.sql`) defining tables (e.g., `sop_sections`, `sop_steps`), unique constraints, and foreign keys with appropriate `ON DELETE` behaviors.
2. **Logic & Triggers**: Update trigger functions (e.g., `freeze_project_templates`) to handle new logic and attach `updated_at` triggers to all new tables.
3. **Security**: Apply Row Level Security (RLS) policies for `authenticated` roles and organization-based restrictions.
4. **Cache Refresh**: Include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` at the end of the script to refresh the PostgREST cache.

### 2. Post-Migration Verification Suite
Execute these queries via `psql` or the Supabase SQL Editor to confirm successful application:
- **Tables & Columns**: Check `information_schema.tables` for new table names and expected column counts.
- **Indexes**: Query `pg_indexes` where `schemaname = 'public'` to verify performance optimization.
- **RLS Status**: Query `pg_class` for `relname` and `relrowsecurity` to ensure RLS is enabled ('t').
- **RLS Policies**: Query `pg_policies` for `tablename`, `policyname`, and `cmd` to confirm access rules match requirements.
- **Triggers**: Query `information_schema.triggers` for `event_object_table` and `trigger_name` to ensure automation is active.
- **Function Logic**: Query `information_schema.routines` and check `routine_definition` using `LIKE` to ensure specific logic (e.g., new column handling) was correctly injected into existing functions.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase. A specific issue was identified where the work orders dashboard showed duplicate trade templates (e.g., 'Painting' appearing multiple times) because the API returned templates from all scopes (app, company, and project) simultaneously.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Scoped Template Retrieval**: Modify the API handler in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` for `GET /templates` to accept an optional `scope` query parameter and apply it to the Supabase query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.
- **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
- **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
- **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **API Client Update**: Update the frontend API client in `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` to support the scope parameter: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? "?scope=${scope}" : ''; return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>( "/templates${query}"); }`.
- **Dashboard Component**: Update `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` to specifically request company-scope templates: `const [templates] = createResource<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Scoped Query Logic:**
```typescript
workOrdersRoute.get('/templates', async (c) => {
    const scope = c.req.query('scope');
    let query = supabase.from('work_order_templates').select('*').eq('is_active', true);
    if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }
    const { data } = await query.order('trade_name');
    return c.json(data);
});
```

**Frontend API Client:**
```typescript
async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') {
    const query = scope ? `?scope=${scope}` : '';
    return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(`/templates${query}`);
}
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Duplicate Dashboard Entries**: Caused by API returning all scopes. Ensure the frontend explicitly passes `?scope=company`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""SOP Library Manager — Supabase Storage + Database Sync

Manages the SOP library lifecycle:
  - Upload .md files to Supabase Storage (apps/voicelog/sops/)
  - Parse SOP markdown into structured data
  - Sync parsed data to database tables (sops, sop_executive_checklists, sop_detailed_steps, sop_recommended_videos)
  - Parse into new normalized schema (sop_sections, sop_steps)
  - Parse REF docs into sop_ref_docs table
  - Extract SOP dependencies from Scope sections
  - Detect changes via MD5 hashing
  - Search/query the library
  - Request agent creation of missing SOPs

Usage:
  python sop_manager.py upload   <file.md>              Upload SOP to storage + parse + sync DB
  python sop_manager.py sync     [--all | <sop_number>]  Re-parse and sync DB from storage
  python sop_manager.py search   <query>                 Search SOPs by keyword/trade/tag
  python sop_manager.py list                              List all SOPs in the library
  python sop_manager.py show     <sop_number>            Show SOP details with cached data
  python sop_manager.py request  <trade> <task>           Request agent to create a new SOP
  python sop_manager.py check    <trade> <task>           Check if suitable SOP exists, request if not
  python sop_manager.py sync-new [--all | <sop_number>]  Sync to new schema (sop_sections + sop_steps)
  python sop_manager.py upload-ref <file.md>             Upload REF doc to sop_ref_docs
  python sop_manager.py sync-deps [--all | <sop_number>] Extract and sync SOP dependencies
"""

import os
import re
import json
import hashlib
import argparse
from datetime import datetime, timezone

except ImportError:

    os.system("pip install supabase")

SUPABASE_KEY = os.environ.get("SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY",

STORAGE_BUCKET = "apps"
STORAGE_PREFIX = "voicelog/sops"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Section type classification keywords

SECTION_TYPE_MAP = {

    "scope": "scope",

    "references and codes": "references",
    "references": "references",

}


def get_client() -> Client:
    return create_client(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_KEY)


def md5_hash(content: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.md5(content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


def classify_section_type(title: str) -> str:
    """Classify a section title into a section_type enum value.

    Matches against SECTION_TYPE_MAP using longest-match-first strategy.
    Falls back to keyword substring matching for procedure sections.
    """
    title_lower = title.lower().strip()

    # Strip leading "Procedure — " or "Procedure - " prefix for matching
    # but still classify as procedure

mber", sop_number).execute()
        if not sop.data:
            return {"status": "error", "message": f"SOP {sop_number} not found in database"}
        try:
            data = self.client.storage.from_(STORAGE_BUCKET).download(sop.data[0]["storage_path"])
            content = data.decode("utf-8")
            file_hash = md5_hash(content)
            return self._sync_from_content(content, sop.data[0]["storage_path"], file_hash)
        except Exception as e:
            return {"status": "error", "message": str(e)}

    def search(self, query: str) -> list:
        """Search SOPs by keyword in sop_number, trade, task, description, tags."""
        q = query.lower()
        results = self.client.table("sops").select("*").or_(
            f"sop_number.ilike.%{q}%,trade_name.ilike.%{q}%,task_name.ilike.%{q}%,description.ilike.%{q}%"
        ).execute()
        return results.data

    def list_all(self) -> list:
        """List all SOPs."""
        results = self.client.table("sops").select(
            "sop_number, trade_code, trade_name, task_name, revision, status, storage_path"
        ).order("sop_number").execute()
        return results.data

    def show(self, sop_number: str) -> dict:
        """Show full SOP details with cached data."""
        sop = self.client.table("sops").select("*").eq("sop_number", sop_number).execute()
        if not sop.data:
            return {"error": f"SOP {sop_number} not found"}
        sop_id = sop.data[0]["id"]
        checklists = self.client.table("sop_executive_checklists").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("section_number,step_number").execute()
        steps = self.client.table("sop_detailed_steps").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("section_number,step_number").execute()
        videos = self.client.table("sop_recommended_videos").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("sort_order").execute()

        # Also include new schema data if available
        sections = self.client.table("sop_sections").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("section_number").execute()
        sop_steps = self.client.table("sop_steps").select("*").eq("sop_id", sop_id).order("phase_number,step_number").execute()
        deps_from = self.client.table("sop_dependencies").select("*, to_sop:sops!sop_dependencies_to_sop_id_fkey(sop_number, task_name)").eq("from_sop_id", sop_id).execute()
        deps_to = self.client.table("sop_dependencies").select("*, from_sop:sops!sop_dependencies_from_sop_id_fkey(sop_number, task_name)").eq("to_sop_id", sop_id).execute()

        return {
            "sop": sop.data[0],
            "executive_checklists": checklists.data,
            "detailed_steps": steps.data,
            "recommended_videos": videos.data,
            "sections": sections.data,
            "sop_steps": sop_steps.data,
            "depends_on": deps_from.data,
            "depended_by": deps_to.data,
        }

    def check_and_request(self, trade: str, task: str) -> dict:
        """Check if a suitable SOP exists. If not, return a request payload for agent creation."""
        results = self.search(f"{trade} {task}")
        if results:
            return {
                "found": True,
                "matches": results,
                "message": f"Found {len(results)} matching SOP(s)"
            }
        # No match — generate agent request
        trade_code = trade[:3].upper()
        # Find next number
        existing = self.client.table("sops").select("sop_number").ilike("sop_number", f"{trade_code}%").execute()
        max_num = 0
        for s in existing.data:
            m = re.search(r"(\d+)$", s["sop_number"])
            if m:
                max_num = max(max_num, int(m.group(1)))
        next_number = f"{trade_code}-SOP-{max_num + 1:03d}"

        request_payload = {
            "found": False,
            "message": f"No SOP found for '{trade} \u2014 {task}'. Agent creation requested.",
            "agent_request": {
                "action": "create_sop",
                "sop_number": next_number,
                "trade_code": trade_code,
                "trade_name": trade,
                "task_name": task,
                "prompt": (
                    f"Create a complete Standard Operating Procedure for {trade} \u2014 {task}. "
                    f"Assign SOP number {next_number}. Follow the SOP Authoring Guide (SOP-META-001) exactly. "
                    f"Include all mandatory sections: Purpose, Scope, References & Codes, Personnel, Safety, "
                    f"Tools & Equipment, Pre-Work Assessment, Procedure phases with Executive Checklists and "
                    f"Detailed Steps, Recommended Videos, Testing & Commissioning, Cleanup, Troubleshooting, "
                    f"Quality Standards, and Revision History. "
                    f"Save the file as {next_number.replace('-', '_')}_{task.replace(' ', '_')}.md"
                )
            }
        }

        # Log the request
        self._log_action(None, next_number, "agent_requested",
                         {"trade": trade, "task": task})
        return request_payload

    def _log_action(self, sop_id, sop_number, action, details=None):
        """Log a sync action."""
        try:
            self.client.table("sop_sync_log").insert({
                "sop_id": sop_id,
                "sop_number": sop_number,
                "action": action,
                "details": details or {}
            }).execute()
        except Exception:
            pass  # non-critical


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="SOP Library Manager")
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")

    # upload
    p_upload = sub.add_parser("upload", help="Upload SOP .md file to storage and sync DB")
    p_upload.add_argument("file", help="Path to .md file")

    # sync
    p_sync = sub.add_parser("sync", help="Re-sync DB from storage")
    p_sync.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="Sync all SOPs")
    p_sync.add_argument("sop_number", nargs="?", help="Specific SOP number to sync")

    # sync-new (new schema)
    p_sync_new = sub.add_parser("sync-new", help="Sync to new schema (sop_sections + sop_steps)")
    p_sync_new.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="Sync all SOPs")
    p_sync_new.add_argument("sop_number", nargs="?", help="Specific SOP number to sync")

    # upload-ref
    p_ref = sub.add_parser("upload-ref", help="Upload REF doc to sop_ref_docs")
    p_ref.add_argument("file", help="Path to REF .md file")

    # sync-deps
    p_deps = sub.add_parser("sync-deps", help="Extract and sync SOP dependencies")
    p_deps.add_argument("--all", action="store_true", help="Sync all SOPs")
    p_deps.add_argument("sop_number", nargs="?", help="Specific SOP number")

    # search
    p_search = sub.add_parser("search", help="Search SOPs")
    p_search.add_argument("query", help="Search query")

    # list
    sub.add_parser("list", help="List all SOPs")

    # show
    p_show = sub.add_parser("show", help="Show SOP details")
    p_show.add_argument("sop_number", help="SOP number")

    # request
    p_req = sub.add_parser("request", help="Request agent to create SOP")
    p_req.add_argument("trade", help="Trade name")
    p_req.add_argument("task", help="Task name")

    # check
    p_check = sub.add_parser("check", help="Check if SOP exists, request if not")
    p_check.add_argument("trade", help="Trade name")
    p_check.add_argument("task", help="Task name")

    args = parser.parse_args()
    mgr = SOPLibraryManager()

    if args.command == "upload":
        result = mgr.upload_sop(args.file)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "sync":
        if args.all:
            results = mgr.sync_all()
            for r in results:
                print(json.dumps(r, indent=2, default=str))
        elif args.sop_number:
            result = mgr.sync_one(args.sop_number)
            print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))
        else:
            print("Specify --all or a specific SOP number")

    elif args.command == "sync-new":
        if args.all:
            results = mgr.sync_new_schema_all()
            for r in results:
                print(json.dumps(r, indent=2, default=str))
        elif args.sop_number:
            result = mgr.sync_new_schema_one(args.sop_number)
            print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))
        else:
            print("Specify --all or a specific SOP number")

    elif args.command == "upload-ref":
        result = mgr.upload_ref_doc(args.file)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "sync-deps":
        if args.all:
            results = mgr.sync_dependencies_all()
            for r in results:
                print(json.dumps(r, indent=2, default=str))
        elif args.sop_number:
            result = mgr.sync_dependencies_one(args.sop_number)
            print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))
        else:
            print("Specify --all or a specific SOP number")

    elif args.command == "search":
        results = mgr.search(args.query)
        for r in results:
            print(f"  {r['sop_number']:15s} {r['trade_name']:15s} {r['task_name']:30s} [{r['status']}]")
        if not results:
            print("  No SOPs found.")

    elif args.command == "list":
        results = mgr.list_all()
        print(f"{'SOP Number':15s} {'Trade':10s} {'Task':30s} {'Rev':5s} {'Status':10s}")
        print("-" * 75)
        for r in results:
            print(f"{r['sop_number']:15s} {r['trade_code']:10s} {r['task_name']:30s} {r['revision']:5s} {r['status']:10s}")
        if not results:
            print("  Library is empty.")

    elif args.command == "show":
        result = mgr.show(args.sop_number)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "request":
        result = mgr.check_and_request(args.trade, args.task)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    elif args.command == "check":
        result = mgr.check_and_request(args.trade, args.task)
        print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

    else:
        parser.print_help()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

kvps.memories:
Tech stack for voicelog-scaffold: SolidJS frontend, Hono API, Supabase (PostgreSQL), pnpm monorepo.

Project 'voicelog-scaffold' Overview (Hennessy / Team Projects):

Location:
- Agent Environment Path: /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold
- Local Path: C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold

Context & Identity:
- The project 'Bill Hennessy Place' (alias 'Hennessy') served as the initial development seed.
- Primary Database Tenant: 'Team Projects'.

Architecture & Tech Stack:
- Structure: pnpm monorepo managed with Turbo.
- Frontend: SolidJS (Port 3005). Includes 'tenant-portal' with routes for /tenants, /tenant/:id, and /onboard.
- API: Hono (Port 3000). Located in apps/api, handling onboarding, organization management, and trade template assignment.
- Database/Auth: Supabase (PostgreSQL). Uses a local development environment (http://127.0.0.1:54321).
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL (Port 54322), PostgREST, and Hono API layer.

Key Systems:
- Tenant Onboarding: Full-stack implementation. The /onboard form and Tenant Detail page include industry selection (sourced from trades template table) and description fields. Redirects to work orders login (Port 3004) after success.
- API Endpoints: POST/GET /onboard, GET /onboard/trades, GET/PATCH /onboard/:id, and admin management.
- Database Management: Uses Supabase migrations via 'scripts/apply-migration.ps1' or 'npm run db:migrate --local'.

Work Order (WO) template hierarchy and inheritance system:

1. Hierarchy Tiers:
- System/App Scope (scope='system' or 'app'): Master templates defined in 'supabase/seed.sql'. ID prefix '30000000'.
- Company Scope (scope='company'): Created during onboarding or industry updates. ID prefix '20000000'.
- Project Scope (scope='project'): Specific to individual projects. Templates are frozen (is_frozen=true) when a project activates, copying definitions from parent company templates.

2. Management & Inheritance:
- The onboarding system (apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts) manages templates via the 'work_order_templates' table.
- Industry updates trigger reassignment by deleting existing company-scope templates and copying from app-scope masters.
- Selecting 'General Contractor' assigns all app-scope templates; specific trades assign only matching ones.

3. API & Implementation:
- API/Client: 'GET /templates' and 'ApiClient.getTemplates' support an optional 'scope' query parameter ('app', 'company', or 'project').
- Filtering: The Dashboard component filters for 'company' scope to prevent duplicates.
- ID Logic: Uses 'a0000000' prefix logic for seeding app-scope from company-scope.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
Implement and verify a complex database migration (e.g., SOP/Work Order bridge) in a Supabase/PostgreSQL environment, ensuring schema integrity, RLS security, and cache synchronization.

# Solution

### 1. Migration Implementation
1. **Schema Definition**: Create migration files (e.g., `supabase/migrations/[timestamp]_name.sql`) defining tables (e.g., `sop_sections`, `sop_steps`), unique constraints, and foreign keys with appropriate `ON DELETE` behaviors.
2. **Logic & Triggers**: Update trigger functions (e.g., `freeze_project_templates`) to handle new logic and attach `updated_at` triggers to all new tables.
3. **Security**: Apply Row Level Security (RLS) policies for `authenticated` roles and organization-based restrictions.
4. **Cache Refresh**: Include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` at the end of the script to refresh the PostgREST cache.

### 2. Post-Migration Verification Suite
Execute these queries via `psql` or the Supabase SQL Editor to confirm successful application:
- **Tables & Columns**: Check `information_schema.tables` for new table names and expected column counts.
- **Indexes**: Query `pg_indexes` where `schemaname = 'public'` to verify performance optimization.
- **RLS Status**: Query `pg_class` for `relname` and `relrowsecurity` to ensure RLS is enabled ('t').
- **RLS Policies**: Query `pg_policies` for `tablename`, `policyname`, and `cmd` to confirm access rules match requirements.
- **Triggers**: Query `information_schema.triggers` for `event_object_table` and `trigger_name` to ensure automation is active.
- **Function Logic**: Query `information_schema.routines` and check `routine_definition` using `LIKE` to ensure specific logic (e.g., new column handling) was correctly injected into existing functions.

# Problem
Implementing a multi-tenant onboarding system and organization management interface using SolidJS and Supabase. A specific issue was identified where the work orders dashboard showed duplicate trade templates (e.g., 'Painting' appearing multiple times) because the API returned templates from all scopes (app, company, and project) simultaneously.

# Solution

### 1. Backend API & Database Architecture (Hono/Supabase)
- **Transaction Flow**: Use `service_role` to bypass RLS during onboarding. Create organization and admin user via `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` with `organization_id` in metadata.
- **Scoped Template Retrieval**: Modify the API handler in `apps/api/src/routes/work-orders.ts` for `GET /templates` to accept an optional `scope` query parameter and apply it to the Supabase query: `if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }`.
- **Automatic Assignment (PATCH /onboard/:id)**: Modify the endpoint in `apps/api/src/routes/onboard.ts` to detect industry changes. Fetch the existing industry and compare it with the new payload.
- **Trade Reassignment**: If the industry changed, delete existing `company` scope templates (where `copied_from_template_id` is not null) and trigger `copyTemplatesToOrg`.
- **copyTemplatesToOrg**: If `industry === 'General Contractor'`, copy all active app templates; otherwise, filter by the specific `trade_name`. Insert copies into `work_order_templates` with `scope = 'company'` and the new `organization_id`.
- **Schema Maintenance**: Always include `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';` after migrations to refresh PostgREST cache.

### 2. Frontend Implementation (SolidJS/Tenant Portal)
- **Organization Profile Management**: Update `TenantDetailPage.tsx` to include `industry` and `description` fields.
- **API Client Update**: Update the frontend API client in `apps/work-orders/src/lib/api.ts` to support the scope parameter: `async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') { const query = scope ? "?scope=${scope}" : ''; return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>( "/templates${query}"); }`.
- **Dashboard Component**: Update `apps/work-orders/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` to specifically request company-scope templates: `const [templates] = createResource<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(() => api.getTemplates('company'));`.
- **Save Logic**: Centralize logic by using the `PATCH` API endpoint (`${API}/onboard/${orgId}`) instead of direct Supabase client calls.

### 3. Code Snippets

**Backend Scoped Query Logic:**
```typescript
workOrdersRoute.get('/templates', async (c) => {
    const scope = c.req.query('scope');
    let query = supabase.from('work_order_templates').select('*').eq('is_active', true);
    if (scope) { query = query.eq('scope', scope); }
    const { data } = await query.order('trade_name');
    return c.json(data);
});
```

**Frontend API Client:**
```typescript
async getTemplates(scope?: 'app' | 'company' | 'project') {
    const query = scope ? `?scope=${scope}` : '';
    return this.request<WorkOrderTemplate[]>(`/templates${query}`);
}
```

### 4. Troubleshooting & Data Integrity
- **Duplicate Dashboard Entries**: Caused by API returning all scopes. Ensure the frontend explicitly passes `?scope=company`.
- **Migration Application**: Run `supabase db reset` to apply migrations.

bash>cd /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold && cat supabase/migrations/20260310000001_sop_wo...

-- =============================================================================
-- Migration: SOP ↔ Work Order Bridge
-- Description:
--   1A. sop_sections — unified hierarchical SOP structure (replaces flat checklists/steps)
--   1B. sop_steps — individual trackable steps (one row = one checklist item = one RAG chunk)
--   1C. sop_dependencies — directed graph of SOP relationships
--   1D. work_order_step_progress — step-level completion tracking for unit WOs
--   1E. sop_ref_docs — trade-level reference documents
--   1F. Add ref_doc_id to sops
--   1G. Add sop_step_id to sop_recommended_videos
--   1H. Update freeze_project_templates() to preserve sop_id
--   1I. RLS policies for all new tables
--   1J. Updated_at triggers for all new tables
-- Date: 2026-03-10
-- =============================================================================

-- =============================================================================
-- 1A. sop_sections — structured sections parsed from SOP markdown files
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_sections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    section_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (section_type IN (
        'purpose', 'scope', 'references', 'personnel', 'safety',
        'tools', 'pre_work', 'procedure', 'videos', 'testing',
        'cleanup', 'troubleshooting', 'quality', 'revision_history'
    )),
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    content_md TEXT,
    content_plain TEXT,
    is_ref_pointer BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    ref_section TEXT,
    phase_name TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(sop_id, section_number)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_sections IS 'Structured sections parsed from SOP markdown files';
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_sections_sop ON public.sop_sections(sop_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_sections_type ON public.sop_sections(section_type);

-- =============================================================================
-- 1B. sop_steps — individual trackable steps
--     Each row = one trackable unit of work = one RAG chunk
--     This is the table that work_order_step_progress references
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_steps (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sop_sections(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    phase_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    phase_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    step_number INTEGER NOT NULL,
    step_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    tasks_summary TEXT,
    detail_md TEXT,
    detail_plain TEXT,
    est_duration_minutes INTEGER,
    requires_photo BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    requires_measurement BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(sop_id, phase_number, step_number)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_steps IS 'Individual trackable steps parsed from SOP procedure phases. One row = one checklist item = one RAG chunk.';
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_steps_sop ON public.sop_steps(sop_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_steps_section ON public.sop_steps(section_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_steps_phase ON public.sop_steps(sop_id, phase_number);

-- =============================================================================
-- 1C. sop_dependencies — directed graph of SOP relationships
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_dependencies (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    from_sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    to_sop_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sops(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    link_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (link_type IN (
        'prerequisite', 'sub_procedure', 'cross_reference', 'optional'
    )),
    condition_note TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(from_sop_id, to_sop_id, link_type)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_dependencies IS 'Directed graph of SOP relationships';
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_deps_from ON public.sop_dependencies(from_sop_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sop_deps_to ON public.sop_dependencies(to_sop_id);

-- =============================================================================
-- 1D. work_order_step_progress — step-level completion tracking
--     The bridge table where field workers record completion of each SOP step
--     within a unit work order. The separate timesheet app reads this data.
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.work_order_step_progress (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    work_order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.work_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    sop_step_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.sop_steps(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN (
        'pending', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'skipped', 'blocked'
    )),
    completed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    duration_minutes INTEGER,
    notes TEXT,
    photo_urls TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
    override_detail TEXT,
    is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    organization_id UUID REFERENCES public.organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE(work_order_id, sop_step_id)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.work_order_step_progress IS 'Step-level completion tracking for unit work orders. Duration feeds timesheet app.';
CREATE INDEX idx_wo_step_progress_wo ON public.work_order_step_progress(work_order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_wo_step_progress_step ON public.work_order_step_progress(sop_step_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_wo_step_progress_user ON public.work_order_step_progress(completed_by);
CREATE INDEX idx_wo_step_progress_status ON public.work_order_step_progress(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_wo_step_progress_org ON public.work_order_step_progress(organization_id);

-- =============================================================================
-- 1E. sop_ref_docs — trade-level reference documents that SOPs link to
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.sop_ref_docs (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    doc_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    trade_code TEXT NOT NULL,
    trade_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    trade_id UUID REFERENCES public.trades(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    revision TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '1.0',
    content_md TEXT,
    storage_path TEXT,
    file_hash TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.sop_ref_docs IS 'Trade-level reference documents that SOPs link to via pointers';

-- =============================================================================
-- 1F. Add ref_doc_id to sops table
-- =============================================================================
ALTER TABLE public.sops
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ref_doc_id UUID REFERENCES public.sop_ref_docs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;

-- =============================================================================
-- 1G. Add sop_step_id to sop_recommended_videos
-- =============================================================================
ALTER TABLE public.sop_recommended_videos
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sop_step_id UUID REFERENCES public.sop_steps(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;

-- =============================================================================
-- 1H. Update freeze_project_templates() trigger
--     Ensure sop_id is preserved when freezing project templates
-- =============================================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.freeze_project_templates()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when status changes TO 'active'
  IF NEW.status = 'active' AND (OLD.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'active') THEN
    UPDATE public.work_order_templates pt
    SET
      definition = ct.definition,
      sop_id = COALESCE(pt.sop_id, ct.sop_id),
      is_frozen = true,
      updated_at = now()
    FROM public.work_order_templates ct
    WHERE pt.project_id = NEW.id
      AND pt.scope = 'project'
      AND pt.parent_template_id = ct.id
      AND pt.is_frozen = false
      AND pt.definition IS NULL;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Note: The trigger trg_freeze_project_templates on wo_projects already exists
-- from migration 20260222000002. CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION above updates it
-- in-place — no need to drop/recreate the trigger binding.

-- =============================================================================
-- 1I. RLS Policies
-- =============================================================================

-- sop_sections
ALTER TABLE public.sop_sections ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "sop_sections_select" ON public.sop_sections FOR SELECT
    USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');
CREATE POLICY "sop_sections_sysadmin" ON public.sop_sections FOR ALL
    USING (public.is_sysadmin());

-- sop_steps
ALTER TABLE public.sop_steps ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "sop_steps_select" ON public.sop_steps FOR SELECT
    USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');
CREATE POLICY "sop_steps_sysadmin" ON public.sop_steps FOR ALL
    USING (public.is_sysadmin());

-- sop_dependencies
ALTER TABLE public.sop_dependencies ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "sop_deps_select" ON public.sop_dependencies FOR SELECT
    USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');
CREATE POLICY "sop_deps_sysadmin" ON public.sop_dependencies FOR ALL
    USING (public.is_sysadmin());

-- sop_ref_docs
ALTER TABLE public.sop_ref_docs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "sop_ref_docs_select" ON public.sop_ref_docs FOR SELECT
    USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');
CREATE POLICY "sop_ref_docs_sysadmin" ON public.sop_ref_docs FOR ALL
    USING (public.is_sysadmin());

-- work_order_step_progress
ALTER TABLE public.work_order_step_progress ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "wo_step_progress_tenant" ON public.work_order_step_progress FOR ALL
    USING (organization_id = public.current_user_organization_id());
CREATE POLICY "wo_step_progress_sysadmin" ON public.work_order_step_progress FOR ALL
    USING (public.is_sysadmin());

-- =============================================================================
-- 1J. Updated_at triggers
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TRIGGER update_sop_sections_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.sop_sections
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column();

CREATE TRIGGER update_sop_steps_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.sop_steps
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column();

CREATE TRIGGER update_sop_ref_docs_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.sop_ref_docs
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column();

CREATE TRIGGER update_wo_step_progress_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.work_order_step_progress
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column();

-- =============================================================================
-- Notify PostgREST to reload schema cache
-- =============================================================================
NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';
(venv) root@d2d162d983f0:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold#
